video:video.video:title: Introducing Money Happens: A new AP audio series
video:video.video:description: Listen to Money Happens, a new audio series where the AP follows how people are dealing with debt, housing and rising costs and provides practical tips from financial experts

video:video.video:title: What did you think of the Met Gala fashion this year?
video:video.video:description: What did you think of the Met Gala fashion this year?

video:video.video:title: Kneecap 'thrive in the chaos' with album 'Fenian' shaped by court case | AP interview
video:video.video:description: The Irish hip-hop group Kneecap recorded their second album "Fenian" while dealing with a legal battle. Liam Óg Ó hAnnaidh, known as Mo Chara, faced terror-related charges for allegedly displaying a Hezbollah flag during a London concert.

video:video.video:title: Billie Eilish premieres concert film with James Cameron — and confetti
video:video.video:description: Billie Eilish and James Cameron pose at the Los Angeles premiere of her "Hit Me Hard and Soft" concert film — then the confetti hits.

video:video.video:title: With new album on the way, Shania Twain hosts ACM Awards | AP interview
video:video.video:description: With an album on the way, Shania Twain is hosting the Academy of Country Music Awards in Las Vegas on May 17.

video:video.video:title: Watch a dramatic thunderstorm sweep across northwestern Kansas
video:video.video:description: Timelapse footage shows a thunderstorm as it sweeps across northwestern Kansas.

video:video.video:title: Why many adopted kids end up in residential treatment centers, an AP investigation
video:video.video:description: An Associated Press investigation finds a business known for tough-love boarding schools for rebellious teenagers has set its sights on a different demographic: adopted kids.

video:video.video:title: Ziggy Marley on legacy, philanthropy and new album | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Ziggy Marley is looking to the "Brightside." The Grammy-winning 57-year-old musician focused his ninth solo studio album on optimism and mental health, along with nods to legacy.

video:video.video:title: Cannes Film Festival opens with Moore, Jackson, Fonda on red carpet
video:video.video:description: The 2026 Cannes Film Festival kicked off with a red carpet featuring Demi Moore, Stellan Skarsgård, Jane Fonda, Tyrese Gibson and director Peter Jackson, who was given an honorary Palme d’Or at the opening ceremony.

video:video.video:title: Cannes 2026: Top moments from opening day
video:video.video:description: The 79th Cannes Film Festival launched on Tuesday, marking the start of 12 days of nonstop premieres that will culminate May 23 with the presentation of the prestigious Palme d’Or.

video:video.video:title: 'Dirtnado' sweeps through Montana farm
video:video.video:description: A "dirtnado" was captured sweeping through a northern Montana farm.

video:video.video:title: The Beach Boys on 60 years of 'Pet Sounds' | AP interview
video:video.video:description: The surviving members of The Beach Boys — Mike Love, Al Jardine and Bruce Johnston — reunited at the iconic Capitol Records building in Los Angeles to celebrate the 60th anniversary of "Pet Sounds," the landmark album that was released May 16, 1966.

video:video.video:title: Watch the moment a cuttlefish changes color
video:video.video:description: Underwater footage captures the moment a cuttlefish changed colors from brown to a bright yellow off the coast of Sydney.

video:video.video:title: Cartel drone attacks empty Mexican towns, force hundreds to flee
video:video.video:description: Alleged members of Los Ardillos attacked communities in Mexico's Guerrero with gunfire, arson and drone explosives, forcing hundreds to flee.

video:video.video:title: Long Island Rail Road shuts down as workers strike
video:video.video:description: A marathon day of negotiations that finished early Monday failed to end a strike that shut down the Long Island Rail Road, North America’s largest commuter rail system, setting the stage for a rough start to the work week for about 250,000 commuters.

video:video.video:title: Two Navy jets collide during Idaho air show
video:video.video:description: An air show in Idaho was called off when two Navy jets collided and crashed. The four crew members from both jets safely ejected and the crash is under investigation.

video:video.video:title: Police on Long Island deploy pepper spray on crowd lining up for high demand luxury watch
video:video.video:description: A scuffle erupted between deputies and watch enthusiasts who had camped outside a New York store on Saturday, prompting officers to deploy pepper spray on the crowd.

video:video.video:title: Cade Klubnik excited for opportunity to learn and grow as he begins NFL career
video:video.video:description: Cade Klubnik excited for opportunity to learn and grow as he begins NFL career

video:video.video:title: Landmark 'Brady Bunch' home opens to fans with set replicas and Easter eggs
video:video.video:description: Fans of "The Brady Bunch" can now walk through their TV memories inside the landmark Los Angeles home used for exterior shots on the hit 1970s sitcom.

video:video.video:title: 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Pedro Pascal is taking "Baby Yoda" to the big screen in "The Mandalorian and Grogu." The star spoke with AP entertainment reporter Leslie Ambriz at the "Star Wars" film's red carpet premiere about sharing the suit of his helmeted Din Djarin character with stuntmen and body doubles Lateef Crowder and

video:video.video:title: 'The Boys' ends: Stars on the spinoff and satire | AP interview
video:video.video:description: After five seasons of death, depravity and digs at the capitalistic superhero-industrial complex, “The Boys” has dropped its series finale.

video:video.video:title: 'Love is the New Gangsta' for 6LACK | AP interview
video:video.video:description: For 6LACK, "family is what's cool, being vulnerable is what's cool." The Grammy-nominated rapper and singer-songwriter says "Love is the New Gangsta" represents his most personal — and honest — album to date.

video:video.video:title: Dog rescued after falling off cliff
video:video.video:description: A dog named Bailey was stranded after falling off a cliff top. In challenging conditions, a crew maneuvered a lifeboat as close as possible to the rocks before a crew member carefully entered the water and successfully rescued the dog.

video:video.video:title: Reporter covering Groundhog Day in 1992 meets Bill Murray
video:video.video:description: Reporter Ted Anthony describes meeting Bill Murray while covering Groundhog Day in 1992.

video:video.video:title: US indictment puts Raúl Castro back in the spotlight
video:video.video:description: Raúl Castro is back at the center of U.S.-Cuba tensions after a U.S. indictment accused him in the 1996 shootdown of exile planes.

video:video.video:title: At the Indy 500, tradition and community bring thousands together
video:video.video:description: Every Memorial Day weekend, Speedway, a small town on the west side of Indianapolis, becomes the center of one of the world’s largest sporting events: the Indianapolis 500.

video:video.video:title: Packers coach Matt LaFleur on Josh Jacobs
video:video.video:description: Green Bay Packers running back Josh Jacobs was released from a Wisconsin jail while authorities further investigate allegations of domestic abuse.

video:video.video:title: Tiny Curaçao gets ready for its first World Cup
video:video.video:description: For the first time, Curaçao is competing in the most-watched sporting show on Earth. The island is the smallest country by population to ever qualify for the World Cup.

video:video.video:title: AP photographer recalls ‘top secret’ trip to Baghdad with George W. Bush in 2003
video:video.video:description: AP Photographer Pablo Martínez Monsiváis shares a behind-the-scenes story of his “top secret” trip to Baghdad, Iraq, with President George W.

video:video.video:title: AP’s soccer writers arriving at Puskas Arena ahead of Champions League final
video:video.video:description: Steve Douglas and James Robson have covered 10 Champions League finals between them. As veterans, they know getting to the stadium early is key, before it gets crowded.

video:video.video:title: Argentine investigators in Ushuaia search for source of deadly hantavirus outbreak
video:video.video:description: Argentine investigators, searching for the source of a deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise that set out from the country last month, were trapping rodents in the forests surrounding the southernmost city of Ushuaia on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Venus Williams sparkles on 2026 Met Gala carpet
video:video.video:description: Met Gala co-chair Venus Williams sparkles in a black gown on the 2026 carpet.

video:video.video:title: Hungary’s newly-elected prime minister Péter Magyar meets Meloni in Rome
video:video.video:description: Hungary’s newly-elected prime minister Péter Magyar met his Italian counterpart Giorgia Meloni in Rome on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Building under construction in Philippines collapses, leaving 1 dead and 21 trapped
video:video.video:description: A nine-story building under construction in a city north of the Philippine capital collapsed before dawn Sunday, leaving at least one Malaysian tourist dead and at least 21 mostly workers trapped in the rubble, officials said.

video:video.video:title: What to know about hantavirus and how it spreads
video:video.video:description: Hantaviruses exist throughout the world and are spread mainly by contact with rodents. It's rare and not easy for a hantavirus to spread between people.

video:video.video:title: Overnight strike by Russia on Ukraine's Sumy region causes destruction
video:video.video:description: Russian drones have struck the Ukrainian town of Shostka, in the northeastern Sumy region, according to local authorities.

video:video.video:title: US teen Buddhist lama is now a monk studying in the Himalayan foothills
video:video.video:description: Jalue Dorje grew up a typical American teen, loving video games and football. But he's also an aspiring spiritual leader recognized from an early age by the Dalai Lama as a reincarnated lama.

video:video.video:title: Beer, brass and 'Hau-Ruck' shouts as a Bavarian village raises its new maypole
video:video.video:description: It's May, and in the Bavarian village of Kühbach, residents are marking the first day of the month by raising a single decorated tree trunk known as a maypole, a custom observed across southern Germany, Austria and parts of central Europe.

video:video.video:title: Temperature records toppling as spring heat wave scorches parts of Western Europe
video:video.video:description: Unpredictable and extreme weather are becoming more frequent as Earth’s warming builds. Experts say unprecedented and deadly weather extremes that sometimes strike at abnormal times and in unusual places are putting more people in danger.

video:video.video:title: Trump declines to say if he and Xi discussed Taiwan
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump arrived at the Temple of Heaven with Chinese President Xi Jinping after the pair discussed trade, Taiwan and other differences in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Trump honors fallen service members at Arlington National Cemetery to mark Memorial Day
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump recognized the upcoming 250th anniversary of American independence as a "historic milestone" and noted that "there could be no Independence Day without Memorial Day.”

video:video.video:title: Tennessee man known for posting racist videos gets $1.25 million bond on attempted murder charge
video:video.video:description: A Tennessee man who goes by Chud the Builder and is known for posting racist videos has been given a $1.25 million preliminary bond on attempted murder charges.

video:video.video:title: With so many aging influencers are we in an era of golden old age?
video:video.video:description: Old age is supposed to be when we step back and relax. But it seems many are stepping forward. If you look online, or in the media, in politics, entertainment or business older people appear to be at their peak.

video:video.video:title: Preparations underway at Granadilla port in Tenerife for arrival of hantavirus-stricken ship
video:video.video:description: Spanish authorities were on Saturday preparing to receive more than 140 passengers and crew members on board a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship headed for the Canary Islands, where health officials have said they will perform careful evacuations.

video:video.video:title: Higher costs force Mississippi farmer to raise prices
video:video.video:description: Foot Print Farms CEO Cindy Ayers-Elliott tries to keep her produce affordable, but she has been forced to raise prices as she struggles to make a profit amid higher costs for gas, fertilizer and other essential items.

video:video.video:title: Jury deliberations begin in murder retrial of former Ohio deputy who fatally shot Black man
video:video.video:description: A jury in Ohio has begun deliberating in the retrial of former sheriff's deputy Jason Meade, charged with murder and reckless homicide in the fatal shooting of 23-year-old Casey Goodson Jr.

video:video.video:title: Paul McCartney and Wings members' artifacts on display at exhibit at Rock and Roll Hall of Fame
video:video.video:description: The largest collection of Paul McCartney's personal artifacts ever publicly displayed is part of a new exhibit at the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in Cleveland, centering on his life after the Beatles and his band Wings.

video:video.video:title: Cuba president condemns additional sanctions imposed by Trump administration
video:video.video:description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel has rejected the latest sanctions imposed by the U.S. government against the island — and against anyone who dares to trade with it — and questioned how long the world will continue to tolerate what he considered to be an abusive collective punishment by the U.S. ad

video:video.video:title: Ed Gallrein defeats Thomas Massie in Kentucky, furthering Trump’s hold on GOP
video:video.video:description: Kentucky U.S. Rep. Thomas Massie lost his Republican House primary Tuesday in another test of President Donald Trump’s power over his party after he handpicked challenger and ultimate winner Ed Gallrein to oust the incumbent.

video:video.video:title: Billie Jean King, 82, earns a college degree 65 years after starting at Cal State LA
video:video.video:description: Billie Jean King celebrated earning a college degree 65 years after first taking classes. The 82-year-old sports icon has received her bachelor's in history from Cal State Los Angeles.

video:video.video:title: Trump to undergo medical checkup as scrutiny over his health continues
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump is scheduled to undergo a medical and dental checkup later this month, as the White House says the president continues routine preventive care ahead of turning 80 in June.

video:video.video:title: The Atlanta Stadium gets a makeover ahead of the World Cup
video:video.video:description: Mercedes-Benz Stadium, renamed Atlanta Stadium in accordance with FIFA regulations, is getting a makeover ahead of the World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Princess of Wales visits Italy after announcing her cancer was in remission
video:video.video:description: Britain’s Princess Catherine made her first overseas trip since announcing her cancer was in remission.

video:video.video:title: UK police seek witnesses in an inquiry into possible offenses by ex-Prince Andrew
video:video.video:description: British police are appealing for witnesses as they expand their investigation into potential offenses by Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor. (Produced by Elaine Carroll)

video:video.video:title: New York federal judge bans ICE arrests at Manhattan immigration courts
video:video.video:description: Federal agents can no longer make arrests without exceptional circumstances in and around three Manhattan buildings where immigration proceedings occur, a judge ruled Monday.

video:video.video:title: Acting U.S. Navy secretary says some foreign military sales are being delayed
video:video.video:description: Taiwan hasn't been notified of any pause in a planned $14 billion U.S. arms sale to the self-governing island, a government official said Friday, after the acting U.S.

video:video.video:description: Billie Eilish describes her relationship with older songs, as she joins Nat Wolff, brother Finneas and co-director James Cameron at the premiere her new tour documentary.

video:video.video:title: A new album and intention for country artist Ashley McBryde
video:video.video:description: Country artist Ashley McBryde suffered a severe injury in 2021, falling from a horse in Montana and landing on her head.

video:video.video:description: This photo gallery highlights some of the most compelling images from around the world, published by The Associated Press during the week of 24-30 April 2026

video:video.video:title: Hawaii's Kilauea volcano erupts again
video:video.video:description: Kilauea has periodically sent fountains of lava soaring during more than a year of an on-and-off eruption.

video:video.video:title: Ridglan Beagles find fans in pop star Debbie Gibson and Black Sabbath bass player
video:video.video:description: Pop star Debbie Gibson and Terry “Geezer” Butler, co-founder of the heavy metal band Black Sabbath, praised ongoing efforts to find new homes for roughly 1,500 beagles purchased from dog breeder and research facility Ridglan Farms outside of Madison.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 28
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday May 28h: Multiple attacks reported in Persian Gulf region; Israeli strikes in Lebanon; Search continues for 9 missing in Washington state paper mill; U.S.

video:video.video:title: 2026's most endangered historic places nod to America 250 and the promise of equality for all
video:video.video:description: The Stonewall National Monument, the President's House Site, and the Women's Rights National Historic Park are on a list of the 11 most endangered historic places in the U.S. compiled by the National Trust for Historic Preservation.

video:video.video:title: Macron sings at Armenian state dinner with Pashinyan on drums
video:video.video:description: French President Emmanuel Macron took to the microphone at a state dinner in Yerevan on Monday, with Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan accompanying him on the drums.

video:video.video:title: Funeral in Venezuela for elderly mother of dead prisoner resonates with chants for justice
video:video.video:description: Emotions ran high at the funeral for Carmen Teresa Navas in Venezuela on Tuesday. The 82-year-old mother died days after learning that her son, Víctor Hugo Quero, had died in state custody.

video:video.video:title: Iranian news agency posts new video said to show still closed Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Iran’s Fars news agency posted a video on Monday showing vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. According to a Fars correspondent, the video shows ships in the northern part of the strait, which is still closed.

video:video.video:title: ‘The situation is not yet under control’: Ebola spreads rapidly in eastern Congo
video:video.video:description: In Rwampara, one of the eastern Congolese towns hardest hit by the ongoing Ebola outbreak, healthcare workers in protective gear loaded bodies into coffins in silence on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: IDF releases video of what it says is an operation to dismantle Gaza tunnel routes
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military said Friday that its troops had “dismantled eight underground tunnel routes” spanning more than 2.5 kilometers that were used by Hamas in the southern Gaza Strip.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV calls for robust regulation of AI
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV called on Monday for robust regulation of artificial intelligence and for its developers to work for the common good rather than profit, issuing a sweeping manifesto on safeguarding humankind as the technology impacts everything from work to war.

video:video.video:title: Israeli troops enter southern Lebanese village under intense shelling
video:video.video:description: Israeli troops entered a southern Lebanese village early Friday under intense shelling, pushing deeper into the country as Lebanese and Israeli military officials began direct talks at the Pentagon over the deadly conflict.

video:video.video:title: 'I consider myself lucky': Survivor recounts Modena car attack
video:video.video:description: A man injured in a car-ramming and stabbing attack in Italy’s northern city of Modena said on Monday he considered himself lucky not to have been more seriously injured, and thanked those who came to his aid.

video:video.video:title: US military boards Iranian-flagged oil tanker suspected of trying to breach blockade
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said Wednesday that it boarded an Iranian-flagged oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman that was suspected of trying to violate the American blockade, the latest action by the Trump administration to try to push Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: NASA lays out moon base plans with landers, buggies and drones at the top of the list
video:video.video:description: NASA is already ordering landers, rovers and drones for a sprawling moon base, less than two months after the Artemis II's record-breaking lunar flyaround.

video:video.video:title: Torrential rains trigger severe flooding in district near Turkey’s Black Sea coast
video:video.video:description: The heavy rainfall late on Tuesday caused rivers in the Havza district of Samsun province to overflow, sending waters rushing down streets, carrying vehicles and debris.

video:video.video:title: Iran calls US proposal to the UN on Strait of Hormuz 'flawed' and 'politically motivated'
video:video.video:description: The resolution -- put forth by the U.S. and Bahrain -- is the second attempt to punish Iran for leveraging its hold over the vital waterway since U.S. and Israeli strikes ignited the war in late February.

video:video.video:title: Protesters rally at Alabama Senate as lawmakers weigh redistricting legislation
video:video.video:description: Protesters blocked a hallway outside the Alabama state Senate in Montgomery on Wednesday. Legislation authorizing special Congressional primaries moved on to the Alabama Senate, after the state House passed it.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Rubio says he hopes China talks to Iran about opening the Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Tuesday he hopes Chinese officials talk to Iran about the Strait of Hormuz during a foreign minister's visit this week.

video:video.video:title: Danish recycling artist brings fairy tale-inspired creations to his first museum exhibit
video:video.video:description: Danish recycling artist Thomas Dambo is renown for his wooden troll sculptures that are usually hidden away in unlikely places waiting to be found by his troll hunter fans around the world.

video:video.video:title: Colombians will vote in a high-stakes test of Gustavo Petro’s agenda
video:video.video:description: Colombia will elect a new president and vice president on May 31 in a vote that has been cast as a referendum on outgoing President Gustavo Petro’s policies.

video:video.video:title: Fans line up for Stephen Colbert’s final 'Late Show' taping
video:video.video:description: Fans lined up in the rain outside the Ed Sullivan Theater in New York on Thursday ahead of the final episode of “The Late Show with Stephen Colbert.”

video:video.video:title: Lady Gaga arrives at 'Mayhem Requiem' premiere
video:video.video:description: Lady Gaga arrives to screening of “Apple Music Live: Lady Gaga Mayhem Requiem.”

video:video.video:title: 'There is no ceasefire': Displaced people in Beirut unconvinced by extension to Israel-Hezbollah truce
video:video.video:description: Displaced families living in makeshift camps in Beirut remain skeptical about the 45-day extension of the expiring Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire.

video:video.video:title: Minibus strikes a parked vehicle on a Pakistan highway, killing 17 people
video:video.video:description: A speeding minibus rammed into a vehicle parked along a motorway in northwest Pakistan on Monday, killing 17 people and injuring five others, police and rescue officials said.

video:video.video:title: Filipino girl group BINI say they're 'blessed' to represent their culture
video:video.video:description: BINI say they're "blessed" to "proudly represent" their culture after making history as the first Filipino girl group to perform at Coachella.
video:video.video:tag: Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival

video:video.video:title: AI agent given the chance to manage a cafe in Stockholm, but it's still fine tuning the bread order
video:video.video:description: The coffee might be poured by a human hand, but behind the counter something far less traditional is calling the shots at a new experimental cafe in Stockholm.

video:video.video:title: United Airlines plane strikes a moving truck during landing at Newark
video:video.video:description: A low flying United Airlines plane crashed in a light pole and a moving bakery truck on the New Jersey Turnpike Sunday afternoon after making an attempt to land at Newark Airport.

video:video.video:title: Paul Bettany and Will Sharpe revisit 'Amadeus'
video:video.video:description: Will Sharpe plays Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart in new Starz limited series, "Amadeus." Paul Bettany plays his nemesis, Antonio Salieri, whose envy and bitter obsession with Mozart saw him turn against his friend and colleague.

video:video.video:title: Rubio arrives in India ahead of Quad talks as US tries to reset strained ties
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived in India on Saturday ahead of a meeting next week with his counterparts from India, Australia, and Japan - members of the Indo-Pacific strategic alliance known as the Quad.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong court hears final arguments in former Tiananmen vigil organizers' national security trial
video:video.video:description: Chow Hang-tung and Lee Cheuk-yan, two former leaders of the Hong Kong Alliance in Support of Patriotic Democratic Movements of China, have pleaded not guilty.

video:video.video:title: US health officials detail plans for American passengers on hantavirus-hit cruise ship
video:video.video:description: The U.S. agreed to send a plane to repatriate and quarantine about 17 Americans who are still on the ship who were on board the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship headed for the Canary Islands.

video:video.video:title: Colombia prepares to elect new president amid election security concerns
video:video.video:description: Colombia will elect a new president and vice president in a vote Sunday that has been cast as a referendum on outgoing President Gustavo Petro’s policies.

video:video.video:title: World Cup pitch at Dallas stadium unveiled
video:video.video:description: Officials on Wednesday unveiled the newly installed World Cup pitch inside Dallas Stadium in Arlington, Texas where suspended LED grow lights are being used to help maintain the grass field ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup matches.

video:video.video:title: Demand soars for Israel's battle-tested weapons tech despite criticism of its wartime conduct
video:video.video:description: When Israeli defense officials approached Massivit last year about using its unique 3D printers to make military drone parts, CEO Yossi Azarzar jumped at the chance.

video:video.video:title: Fire breaks out at hotel in Thailand's popular Pattaya area
video:video.video:description: A fire broke out at a hotel in Pattaya, Thailand, on Thursday, injuring four people, according to local media reports.

video:video.video:title: New York City police release footage of machete-wielding man shot at Grand Central
video:video.video:description: New York City police have released body-worn camera footage of officers shooting and killing a machete-wielding man who stabbed three people at a Grand Central subway stop last month.

video:video.video:title: 'We've come up short' Senator John Cornyn said after losing the Texas primary election
video:video.video:description: Sen. John Cornyn conceded defeat in the Texas Senate primary runoff after Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton secured victory in a massively expensive race.

video:video.video:title: Protests in Venezuela after elderly mother dies days after learning missing son died in prison
video:video.video:description: Protesters demonstrated Monday in Venezuela’s capital in memory of a woman who died over the weekend, just days after learning that her son had died in state custody 10 months ago.

video:video.video:title: Landlords seek relief from unpaid rent in lawsuit against federal eviction ban
video:video.video:description: A group of landlords are hoping to settle with the federal government over what they say are billions of dollars in losses due to the federal eviction moratorium in place for part of the pandemic.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: Putin: Russia's conflict with Ukraine 'coming to an end'
video:video.video:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said a U.S.-brokered ceasefire with Ukraine had emerged following discussions with the United States, following the Russian Ministry of Defense's statement about the possibility of retaliatory attacks on Kyiv.

video:video.video:title: Official says San Diego mosque security guard 'delayed' shooters, saving lives
video:video.video:description: San Diego Police Chief Scott Wahl said the security guard of the Islamic Center of San Diego "delayed and distracted" the shooting suspects, which saved lives without a doubt.

video:video.video:title: Candidates for California governor tangle in crowded race ahead of state primary, AP explains
video:video.video:description: Over 50 candidates are vying to replace Democratic Gov. Gavin Newsom, who is barred by law from seeking a third term, to lead the nation’s most populous state and one of the world’s largest economies.

video:video.video:title: Two suspects killed in shooting at a San Diego mosque
video:video.video:description: The two suspects in a Monday shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego are dead, according to a police source.

video:video.video:title: Blunt and Tucci get stars on Walk of Fame
video:video.video:description: Emily Blunt and Stanley Tucci are celebrated by Matt Damon, Robert Downey Jr., Meryl Streep, and Dwayne Johnson, on the Hollywood Walk of Fame.

video:video.video:title: Mid-term elections in Britain could hasten the end for embattled leader Starmer
video:video.video:description: British voters will cast their ballots on Thursday in elections that could hasten the end of Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s troubled term, and confirm that an increasingly dis-United Kingdom has entered an era of messy multiparty politics.

video:video.video:title: Large fire spreads across Chernobyl exclusion zone as landmines hamper firefighting efforts
video:video.video:description: Firefighters were attempting to contain a large fire in the forest of the Chernobyl exclusion zone on Friday, Ukrainian authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Mexico's Sheinbaum hands World Cup tickets to four young Mexican women ahead of tournament
video:video.video:description: Four young Mexican women who won tickets to the 2026 FIFA World Cup met President Claudia Sheinbaum on Friday after a nationwide contest aimed at promoting women’s soccer and giving fans a chance to attend matches in Mexico.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Iran wants peace deal to end war in the Middle East 'more than I do'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump told reporters in Washington that the ceasefire with Iran was holding. Trump also said Iran "trifled with us," after the U.S. military said it intercepted Iranian attacks on three Navy ships in the Strait of Hormuz and “targeted Iranian military facilities responsible for atta

video:video.video:title: Eilish calls Attenborough 'the GOAT'
video:video.video:description: Billie Eilish explains why she admires David Attenborough - who will turn 100 this week - as she promotes her concert movie with iconic director James Cameron.

video:video.video:title: Naming of new species among scores of tributes honoring David Attenborough's 100th birthday
video:video.video:description: A newly discovered parasitic wasp from Chile has been named after Sir David Attenborough ahead of his 100th birthday, one of several tributes marking the broadcaster’s influence on natural history, conservation and environmental awareness.

video:video.video:title: Cruise ship waits for help after suspected outbreak of rare hantavirus onboard kills 3
video:video.video:description: At least three people have died and others have been sickened by what health authorities suspect is a hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean.

video:video.video:title: A converted church in rural Pennsylvania is becoming an incubator for Amish roots music
video:video.video:description: Ragamuffin Hall in McCoysville was the setting last weekend for a concert by two of the rising stars, Ben and Rose Stoltzfus.

video:video.video:title: 14-year-old boy among 6 dead from Honduras and Mexico found in hot Texas shipping container
video:video.video:description: More details are out after six people were found dead in a rail yard shipping container in Laredo, Texas, last weekend.

video:video.video:title: Flotilla activists describe beatings, tasers and mistreatment by Israeli forces
video:video.video:description: Activists detained after their flotilla tried to breach Israel's naval blockade of Gaza have reported mistreatment by Israeli soldiers.

video:video.video:title: Robotics team looks at its creation as a new species, not copy of humans or animals
video:video.video:description: Argus looks more like a virus than a robot, and that's the point. The team at Duke University's General Robotics Lab says they're out to create a new species, not just more copies of humans, dogs or birds.

video:video.video:title: Ukraine's LELÉKA connects with fans ahead of Eurovision's semifinal
video:video.video:description: Eurovision contestant LELÉKA met with members of her native Ukrainian community in Vienna to connect with fans.

video:video.video:title: Kansas City small businesses whipping up World Cup flavors and merch for arriving fans this summer
video:video.video:description: Local businesses around Kansas City are releasing twists to their signature products and other merchandise to join in on the World Cup festivities.

video:video.video:title: Trump: Netanyahu 'will do whatever I want him to do' on Iran
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu “will do whatever I want him to do” on Iran as negotiations continue, a day after saying he was “an hour away” from ordering strikes.

video:video.video:title: Russia continues large-scale nuclear drills
video:video.video:description: Russia on Thursday released a video showing large-scale maneuvers of its nuclear forces “to attain readiness for combat missions involving missile launches,” its Defense Ministry said.

video:video.video:title: World Health Organization expert says hantavirus is not the next COVID
video:video.video:description: A top epidemic expert at the U.N. health agency said Wednesday that the hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship is not like the COVID pandemic.

video:video.video:title: Floral festival takes over wealthy London neighbourhood
video:video.video:description: Shops and public spaces in London's affluent neighborhood are decorated in blooms for an annual celebration that runs parallel to the world-famous Chelsea Flower Show.

video:video.video:title: Otter pups begin swimming lessons at UK zoo
video:video.video:description: Three rare giant otter pups have begun swimming lessons at the UK's Chester Zoo. Led by mum Bonita and dad Manu, the rare pups were picked up by the scruff of their necks and popped straight in at the ‘deep end’.

video:video.video:title: 'The Boys' wraps up in LA
video:video.video:description: Daveed Diggs and the cast of "The Boys" reunite in Los Angeles to celebrate the final season of the hit superhero show.

video:video.video:title: Activist comments as Israeli forces intercept 6 more flotilla vessels headed for Gaza
video:video.video:description: In footage released by the Global Sumud Flotilla, Israeli forces on a vessel were seen intercepting activists at sea.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo marks World Press Freedom Day remembering members of media killed at war
video:video.video:description: The United Nations General Assembly proclaimed May 3 as World Press Freedom Day in 1993. 2025 was the deadliest year for journalists worldwide ever recorded, according to The Committee to Protect Journalists.

video:video.video:title: Shooting at San Diego mosque kills 3 men and both suspects are dead, police say
video:video.video:description: A shooting at a San Diego mosque on Monday killed three men, and two teenage suspects are also dead, the police chief said.

video:video.video:title: Indiana woman formerly prescribed Mifepristone reacts to Supreme Court abortion pill decision
video:video.video:description: For Kristina Moorhead, a woman in Indiana who was prescribed the pills twice to complete two missed miscarriages in 2024, the decision “is incredibly important”.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: 'Is God Is' writer and director Aleshea Harris on how the film explores unapologetic female rage
video:video.video:description: Award-winning playwright Aleshea Harris is bringing "Is God Is" to the screen. The film follows two twin sisters, Racine and Anaia, who embark on a quest for revenge to confront their dark family history.

video:video.video:title: Is Woody Tom Hanks' most iconic character?
video:video.video:description: Tom Hanks says the success of Woody from the Toy Story franchise "blows me out of my hut."

video:video.video:title: Nine people injured in Russian attack on Kharkiv
video:video.video:description: Nine people were injured, including three children, in a Russian strike on Ukraine’s second largest city, Kharkiv, on Thursday, according to Kharkiv Mayor Ihor Terekhov.

video:video.video:title: A fan-run club pushes back against aggressive stadium culture in Poland
video:video.video:description: A grassroots Warsaw soccer club formed by fans more than a decade ago to resist aggressive nationalist stadium culture is hoping to find new relevance in Poland — a country whose president doesn’t deny his own past participation in fights between football fans.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy arrives in Sweden as Ukraine touts drone expertise
video:video.video:description: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy arrived in Sweden on Thursday for talks with Prime Minister Ulf Kristersson on bilateral defense cooperation, the Ukrainian leader and the Swedish government said.

video:video.video:title: Barbara Palvin shows off baby bump with Dylan Sprouse in Cannes
video:video.video:description: Actor Dylan Sprouse and his model wife Barbara Palvin are expecting their first baby. The couple announced the news at the 79th Annual Cannes Film Festival. (May 14)

video:video.video:title: Atlanta Police Department holds mass shooting training ahead of the World Cup
video:video.video:description: The Atlanta Police department is preparing its emergency response in case an incident happens during the World Cup. (AP Video by Emilie Megnien)

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 26
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, May 26th: European Commission spokesperson calls Russia's demand for diplomats to leave Ukraine unacceptable; Netanyahu meets with defense officials after strike in Lebanon; Train and minibus accident in Belgium leaves four dead; Drones drop from sky in Sydney during n

video:video.video:title: Brazilian artisan crafts replica World Cup trophies to the joy of locals and celebrities alike
video:video.video:description: Brazilian soccer fan and former metallurgist Jarbas Meneghini Carlini has been combining his love for the sport with his business, making replicas of the FIFA World Cup trophy in his workshop in western Rio de Janeiro.

video:video.video:title: Prosecutors to retry Alex Murdaugh after murder convictions overturned
video:video.video:description: Alex Murdaugh’s murder convictions and life sentence for the deaths of his wife and son were overturned Wednesday by the South Carolina Supreme Court.

video:video.video:title: Andrew Scott takes on the 'gripping story' of the meteorologist who saved D-Day
video:video.video:description: D-Day was supposed to happen on June 5, 1944, but ultimately took place the next day because of a Scottish meteorologist played by Andrew Scott in new drama "Pressure."

video:video.video:title: Jimmy Lai's son hopes Trump's meeting with Xi could lead to breakthrough to free his father
video:video.video:description: Ahead of U.S. President Donald Trump's anticipated trip to Beijing next week, the family of imprisoned media mogul and Hong Kong's pro-democracy activist Jimmy Lai was hopeful of his release, Sebastien Lai, son of Jimmy Lai, said in an interview with The Associated Press.

video:video.video:title: The Hoover Dam marks America 250
video:video.video:description: The Hoover Dam is marking the United States’ 250th anniversary with a 300 foot tall flag and a lighting display.

video:video.video:title: Iron Maiden on their mascot, Eddie
video:video.video:description: British heavy metal veterans Iron Maiden love to hate their terrifying mascot, Eddie.

video:video.video:title: Why meat-eating dinosaurs like T. rex evolved to have tiny arms
video:video.video:description: Why does such a big and powerful dinosaur like the T. rex have such tiny forearms, in a new study scientists believe they might have the answer.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 17
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Sunday, May 17th: WHO declares an Ebola emergency; A drone strike hits the UAE’s sole nuclear plant; Russian officials say Ukrainian strikes killed four people; An owl sanctuary in Spain promotes wildlife conservation.

video:video.video:title: Catherine Zeta-Jones taps into her motherly instincts to play Morticia Addams
video:video.video:description: Catherine Zeta-Jones says she was able to tap into her own motherly instincts to play Morticia Addams in the Netflix series "Wednesday."

video:video.video:title: What to know about new Ebola outbreak that has killed scores of people in Congo
video:video.video:description: Africa’s top public health body has confirmed a new Ebola outbreak in Congo's Ituri province. There have been 246 suspected cases and 65 deaths so far.

video:video.video:title: States across the wildfire-prone Western U.S. are using AI for early detection
video:video.video:description: AI cameras are playing an increasingly important role in detecting wildfires in the drought-prone U.S. West.

video:video.video:title: Jensen Ackles talks 'The Boys' spin-off at series finale premiere
video:video.video:description: Actor Jensen Ackles shares his hopes that "The Boys" spin-off "Vought Rising" will be as successful as the hit superhero series, as the cast gathered in Los Angeles to celebrate the final episode of the Prime Video show.

video:video.video:title: Colombia discusses hippo relocation with Vantara after backlash over euthanasia plan
video:video.video:description: The Colombian government on Wednesday announced that Indian conservation initiative Vantara had expressed interest in relocating an invasive population of hippopotamuses in the country.

video:video.video:title: Fans paint London streets red with flares to celebrate Arsenal's Premier League victory
video:video.video:description: Thousands of jubilant fans gathered along London's streets on Sunday to celebrate successful seasons for both the Arsenal men's and women's teams.

video:video.video:title: Power outages and fuel shortages deepen frustrations across Cuba
video:video.video:description: Residents in Havana banged pots and pans overnight Thursday into Friday as prolonged power outages and fuel shortages deepened frustrations across Cuba.

video:video.video:title: A Latino grocery store in Delaware turns its produce aisle into a music stage
video:video.video:description: A family-owned Latino grocery store in Delaware is attracting online audiences and bands from far away with monthly concerts from diverse artists and genres.

video:video.video:title: Thousands of volunteers build dozens of homes in a week in Atlanta with Habitat for Humanity
video:video.video:description: Over five days, nearly 2,200 volunteers with the international nonprofit will finish building 24 new housing units in Atlanta along with homeowners.

video:video.video:title: Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship docks in the Netherlands for disinfection as final crew disembark
video:video.video:description: A cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak docked at the Dutch port of Rotterdam for disinfection Monday, wrapping up a troubled journey that put international health authorities on alert.

video:video.video:title: Russia ramps up attempts to kill opponents in Europe, intelligence officials say
video:video.video:description: While Russian officials have long been accused of silencing the country’s enemies abroad, three Western intelligence officials from different countries told AP that a campaign of targeted killings has ramped up since President Vladimir Putin's 2022 invasion of Ukraine.

video:video.video:title: Pam Bondi faces House questioning over Epstein files release
video:video.video:description: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi is testifying before House lawmakers investigating Jeffrey Epstein's sexual abuse cases, a long-awaited appearance that brings fresh scrutiny of the Trump administration's botched release of the Epstein case files.

video:video.video:title: Groups rally to defend Black political representation in Alabama
video:video.video:description: Thousands of people gathered in Montgomery, Alabama, to mobilize support for voting rights amid concerns about states that are moving to dismantle congressional districts that expanded representation opportunities.

video:video.video:title: Turkish police storm offices of main opposition CHP party, firing tear gas and rubber bullets
video:video.video:description: Police stormed the offices of Turkey’s main opposition CHP party on Sunday, firing tear gas and rubber bullets at party supporters and officials who had been holed up inside for three days.

video:video.video:title: Loud booms heard in Massachusetts after meteor enters atmosphere near border with New Hampshire
video:video.video:description: The American Meteor Society said a boom heard by residents in multiple states Saturday afternoon was a 3-foot wide meteor entering the atmosphere near the Massachusetts and New Hampshire border.

video:video.video:title: Ebola treatment center burns in eastern Congo city of Rwampara
video:video.video:description: The Ebola treatment center in Rwampara in the Democratic Republic of Congo, burned Thursday as gunshots could be heard nearby and local residents were seen chasing away and hitting a white four-wheel drive vehicle often used by NGOs in the area.

video:video.video:title: Uganda puts more than 100 people in quarantine while Congo sets up Ebola treatment centers
video:video.video:description: The Ugandan government has confirmed that over 100 people are being quarantined at an undisclosed location as they are monitored for signs and symptoms of a rare type of Ebola virus.

video:video.video:title: Small medical plane crashes in New Mexico, killing all 4 people aboard
video:video.video:description: A small medical plane crashed in a mountain range outside Ruidoso, New Mexico, killing all four people aboard and sparking a wildfire in the surrounding forest.

video:video.video:title: Displaced Palestinians speak of their struggles as they face another grim Eid al-Adha
video:video.video:description: Palestinians across the Gaza Strip are facing another grim Eid al-Adha, or “Feast of Sacrifice”, an Islamic holiday celebrated by millions of Muslims across the globe.

video:video.video:title: Sharon Stone, Demi Moore bring glamour to Cannes
video:video.video:description: Cannes Film Festival’s crazy red carpet schedule continues - and with it, some stand-out looks from Sharon Stone, Demi Moore and Cate Blanchett. (May 20)

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 5
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, May 5th: Defense department says ceasefire with Iran still in effect; Romanian government falls as no-confidence motion against Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan passes; Book publishers sue Meta; Tony Award nominations announced.

video:video.video:title: Meet Tornado and Twister, the two kittens rescued after tornado in Mississippi
video:video.video:description: Two aptly-named kittens, Tornado and Twister, who were rescued from the rubble of a tornado-flattened Mississippi trailer park, now have a new family.

video:video.video:title: High-speed passenger hydrofoil boat makes its first seasonal voyage from Sochi to Sukhumi
video:video.video:description: A high-speed passenger vessel "Kometa" made its first voyage of the year on Friday from Russia's Sochi to Sukhumi in Abkhazia, Georgia.

video:video.video:title: Brendan Fraser says starring in 'Pressure' taught him more about D-Day
video:video.video:description: Brendan Fraser got a humbling history lesson while starring in the World War II drama "Pressure." He plays Gen. Dwight D. Eisenhower in the film.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 18
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, May 18th: Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship docks in Rotterdam; Luigi Mangione in court for evidence hearing; Turkey's Erdogan denounces Israeli navy's interception of Gaza-bound flotilla; Epstein files exhibition opens in New York City.

video:video.video:title: Drones, bullets and cartel warfare fuel an invisible displacement crisis in Mexico
video:video.video:description: María Cabrera and her family fled into the night-cloaked mountains of central Mexico with only the clothes on their backs when bombs fell from the sky and bullets ricocheted off her concrete floors.

video:video.video:title: Trump offers platitudes while Xi warns of possible confrontation during China summit
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump has called Chinese President Xi Jinping a friend, but his warm words were in stark contrast to Xi, who opened a bilateral meeting by cautioning about potential conflict between the U.S. and China.

video:video.video:title: Deaths reported after tank implodes at Washington pulp and paper mill
video:video.video:description: The implosion of a chemical tank at a pulp and paper mill in southwestern Washington state on Tuesday killed an undisclosed number of people, local authorities and company officials confirmed.

video:video.video:title: Hospitals in southern Lebanon face uphill struggle as they treat the wounded and displaced
video:video.video:description: Hospitals in southern Lebanon are under immense strain and overwhelmed due to the dual pressures of war injuries and displacement. (AP Video: Bassam Hatoum

video:video.video:title: Convoy of oil trucks shows war-battered Syria selling itself as safe corridor amid regional conflict
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of oil-laden trucks, some arriving from Iraq, lined up along Syria's coastal road on Friday as they wait to unload into tankers at Baniyas port.

video:video.video:title: Kneecap sampled court case crowds on new record
video:video.video:description: How Kneecap member Mo Chara's recent court case helped to inspire a song on the Irish rappers' second album, "Fenian."

video:video.video:title: Rome’s ‘sexy priest’ calendar star never set foot in a seminary
video:video.video:description: A calendar featuring close-ups of young, handsome priests has been a popular Rome souvenir for two decades. However, many of those photographed aren't actually priests.

video:video.video:title: Europe faces stray Ukrainian drones as Kyiv targets Russian oil exports
video:video.video:description: Stray Ukrainian drones have been breaching NATO airspace as Kyiv targets Russia's oil exports. On Wednesday, Lithuanians sheltered in Vilnius after an unidentified drone crossed the border.

video:video.video:title: Interfaith march in Jerusalem's Old City seeks to provide show of unity
video:video.video:description: Dozens of activists participated in an interfaith march through Jerusalem on Monday. (AP Video by Shlomo Mor)

video:video.video:title: Trump marks Memorial Day at Arlington National Cemetery
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump marked Memorial Day with a wreath-laying ceremony at Arlington National Cemetery alongside Vice President JD Vance and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.

video:video.video:title: Port Authority in New York tests drone deliveries between Manhattan and Brooklyn
video:video.video:description: The trial targets a year-end goal of delivering medical supplies, and eventually expanding to other routes. (AP video by Emily Wang Fujiyama)
video:video.video:tag: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

video:video.video:title: A timeline of US-Cuba relations as tensions rise in Trump's second term
video:video.video:description: The U.S. indictment of former Cuban President Raúl Castro is the latest salvo in the Trump administration’s months-long pressure campaign against the Caribbean island's socialist-controlled government.

video:video.video:title: Funeral held in Gaza for leader of Hamas’ military wing killed in Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: Mourners in the Gaza Strip held a funeral procession for the leader of Hamas’ military wing on Saturday after he was killed in an Israeli airstrike the previous day.

video:video.video:title: Why is everything so expensive? Business basics: Inflation
video:video.video:description: When the cost of nearly everything is rising, economists call it inflation. Even if paychecks also rise, inflation typically leads people feel like it's harder to make ends meet, and it often becomes a major political issue

video:video.video:title: Police search for suspects in Oklahoma shooting that sent at least 18 people to hospitals
video:video.video:description: Authorities in Oklahoma were looking for suspects Monday in a weekend shooting at an unsanctioned lakeside party packed with young adults that left at least 23 people injured, some critically, according to police and hospital officials.

video:video.video:title: A$AP Rocky on the Met Gala carpet: 'We feel how we look'
video:video.video:description: Rihanna and A$AP Rocky made a joint arrival at the Met Gala, closing out the carpet in signature fashion. Reflecting on their look, Rocky said, “We feel how we look.”

video:video.video:title: In Iran's capital, an influx of weaponry signals those at home and abroad as war threats remain
video:video.video:description: Weapons are now regularly brandished in Tehran, an increasing show of defiance as U.S. President Donald Trump threatens he could restart the war with Iran should negotiations break down and the Islamic Republic refuses to release its grip on the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Fuerzas israelíes interceptan una flotilla que intenta romper el bloqueo a Gaza
video:video.video:description: Las fuerzas armadas israelíes interceptaron botes frente a la costa de Chipre este lunes, como parte de la más reciente ola de flotillas de activistas que intentan romper el bloqueo naval de Israel sobre Gaza.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers free 4 men trapped in flooded Laos cave, search for 2 still missing
video:video.video:description: Rescue workers in Laos said Saturday they have safely evacuated four villagers trapped in a flooded cave for 10 days, the day after another one was successfully extracted.

video:video.video:title: Protesters light bonfires during public transport strike in Kenya over fuel prices
video:video.video:description: Protests have erupted in Nairobi as a nationwide public transport strike begins over rising fuel prices. (AP video shot by Andrew Kasuku)

video:video.video:description: In Wümme, Germany, on Thursday, U.S.-based Echodyne and Germany-based Argus Interception demonstrated their latest tech for the media.

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday he was “an hour away” from ordering strikes on Iran before Gulf allies urged him to pause, saying they were close to a deal.

video:video.video:description: Israeli drones struck three vehicles on the main highway south of Beirut Wednesday, killing eight people, including a woman and her two children, the Lebanese Health Ministry said.

video:video.video:title: Blunt and Tucci get stars on Walk of Fame
video:video.video:description: Emily Blunt and Stanley Tucci are celebrated by Matt Damon, Robert Downey Jr., Meryl Streep, and Dwayne Johnson, on the Hollywood Walk of Fame

video:video.video:title: Thalia reflects on friendship with Michael Jackson
video:video.video:description: While promoting her recent album, "Todo Suena Mejor en Cumbia," Thalia said her friendship with Michael Jackson was "beautiful" and says the two stars bonded over their shared experiences growing up in the global spotlight.

video:video.video:title: Kouri Richins, who wrote a children’s book on grief after her husband’s death, gets life sentence
video:video.video:description: Kouri Richins was convicted in March of aggravated murder for lacing her husband Eric Richins’ cocktail with five times the lethal dose of fentanyl at their home near Park City in 2022.

video:video.video:title: Matthew Perry's parents arrive for sentencing of assistant over his death
video:video.video:description: Matthew Perry’s live-in personal assistant, who had a central role in the “Friends” actor’s descent into ketamine addiction and injected him with a fatal dose of the drug, was sentenced Wednesday to three years and five months in prison, bringing an end to the legal saga surrounding the death of one

video:video.video:title: At least five people killed and 38 injured in mall fire in northwestern Mexico
video:video.video:description: At least five people were killed and another 38 injured on Thursday after a fire broke out at a shopping mall in the Mexican city of Los Mochis, state authorities reported.

video:video.video:title: Trump invites Xi to visit White House in September during banquet toast in Beijing
video:video.video:description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and U.S. President Donald Trump each proposed a toast during a state dinner at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians in Gaza say mass displacement suffered by their predecessors on Nakba Day continues today
video:video.video:description: Israeli bombardment and mass displacement in Gaza have left many Palestinians comparing the current war to the Nakba of 1948, when hundreds of thousands were forced from their homes during the creation of Israel.

video:video.video:title: Gigi Hadid shares what the Met Gala means to her
video:video.video:description: Model Gigi Hadid tells AP about the Meta Gala and fashion education.

video:video.video:title: Long DACA renewal wait times leave some 'Dreamers' without status, a job and fearing detainment
video:video.video:description: Renewal wait times for the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals program that allows people who were brought to the U.S. as children to temporarily remain in the country and work have increased dramatically in the past year.

video:video.video:title: Families evacuated from Gaza enjoy a day to decompress at Rome's ancient baths
video:video.video:description: The Baths of Caracalla, the sumptuously decorated public baths complex near the Colosseum, were long a place of leisure, health care and relaxation for ancient Romans.

video:video.video:title: At least one dead in Russian strike on kindergarten in Ukraine's Sumy
video:video.video:description: One person was killed and two injured in a Russian strike on a kindergarten in in Ukraine's northeastern city of Sumy on Wednesday, Ukrainian State Emergency Service said.

video:video.video:description: Police raced Monday to catch an armed teenage runaway before he and another teen opened fire on a San Diego mosque, killing three men and then themselves.

video:video.video:title: Health officials track dozens who left hantavirus-stricken ship after first fatality
video:video.video:description: Health officials in several countries are trying to identify and follow people who may have been exposed to hantavirus on a cruise ship. (Production by Elaine Carroll)

video:video.video:title: Migrant amateur teams in Greece get into World Cup spirit before new EU border measures take effect
video:video.video:description: Migrant and refugee teams in Greece's capital took part in a grassroots soccer tournament before the World Cup in North America.

video:video.video:description: Paris Saint-Germain’s second Champions League title win was marred by violence as people disrupted celebrations and a group tried to storm a police station in the French capital.

video:video.video:title: K-pop stars IDID share best advice
video:video.video:description: Members of Kpop outfit IDID reveal the best advice they have received from fellow musicians. (May 27)

video:video.video:title: A handful of women who never heard of handball now are training to join the US Olympic team
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of women have tried out for a chance to join the U.S. women’s handball team for the 2028 Olympics.

video:video.video:description: President Trump has lambasted many of his allies for failing in his eyes to adequately support his war in Iran but America’s closest, the U.K., has drawn his particular ire.

video:video.video:title: How 'Breaking Bad' helped Nicolas Cage see the possibilities in television
video:video.video:description: Nicolas Cage says working on “Spider-Noir” as a series gave him more creative freedom than film. (May 13)

video:video.video:title: PM Netanyahu announces 'deepening' of Israeli military operation in Lebanon
video:video.video:description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Tuesday that he had ordered a "deepening" of his country's military operation in Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: Yuji the baby monkey finds comfort in plush teddy
video:video.video:description: Yuji, the baby patas monkey, is winning hearts and fans for his love of a stuffed teddy bear. Little Yuji was rejected by his mother at birth who was not able to care for him, so now he is receiving the love and care from the expert staff at Guadalajara Zoo in Mexico.

video:video.video:description: Red Cross volunteer Vanny Birungi walks through neighborhoods in Bunia, Democratic Republic of Congo, every day warning residents about Ebola amid an outbreak that has triggered fear, misinformation and attacks on aid workers.

video:video.video:description: Top officials in the U.S. Justice Department and Department of Agriculture said the administration is seeking whistleblowers to combat anti-competitive practices in the beef industry.

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump showed reporters the White House ballroom construction site Tuesday, defending the project and touting its security features after $1B in security funding for the project was struck down.

video:video.video:description: The New York Botanical Garden’s summer exhibit explores the cultural and environmental impact of 1960s flower power. (AP Video: Ted Shaffrey)

video:video.video:description: DRC health minister Samuel Roger Kamba said on Sunday that authorities had already selected sites in Rwampara and Mongwalu to establish new treatment centers and that dozens of patients were receiving active care.

video:video.video:title: A solar-powered charging station in central Cuba brings life to a darkened island
video:video.video:description: A free, solar-powered charging station, or “solinera” as it’s known, has opened in Santa Clara, in Cuba’s central region.

video:video.video:title: Former Patriots receiver Stefon Diggs is found not guilty of assaulting his private chef
video:video.video:description: Former Patriots receiver Stefon Diggs has been found not guilty of assaulting his private chef in a pay dispute.
