video:video.video:description: Colombia is voting in its first round of presidential elections over the weekend in a race that has candidates grappling with the larger question of how to consolidate peace.

video:video.video:title: Humanoid robots and manual dexterity showcase in Japan humanoids summit
video:video.video:description: Mechanical hands claimed to be dexterous enough to thread a needle, childlike dancing robots and adult-sized ones to help with deliveries - they're all being presented as a future part of the workforce at Tokyo's Humanoids Summit.

video:video.video:title: Latvian prime minister resigns after controversy over stray Ukrainian drones
video:video.video:description: Latvian center-right Prime Minister Evika Silina announced her resignation on Thursday, after the Progressives Party, her left-leaning coalition partner, pulled support from the government and left her without a majority.

video:video.video:title: Congressman promises to sue over Tennessee's new US House map
video:video.video:description: Democratic Rep. Steve Cohen and the NAACP Memphis Branch are promising to fight the new U.S. House map that carves up Memphis' majority-Black congressional district and was approved by Republican lawmakers on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Starmer comments after partial results show losses for Labour, wins for Reform UK in local elections
video:video.video:description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer insisted he would carry on as prime minister despite his Labour party suffering disastrous local election losses.

video:video.video:title: Demi Moore, Lucas Bravo among guests at Women in Cinema Gala
video:video.video:description: The Red Sea Film Foundation's annual gala in Cap d'Antibes, South of France, honored Laïla Marrakchi, Genevieve Nnaji, Tara Sutaria, Marie-Clementine Dusabejambo, Kamila Andani and Aixa Kay.

video:video.video:title: A de-extinction company has hatched live chicks from an artificial eggshell
video:video.video:description: Colossal Biosciences says 26 baby chickens were born from a 3D printed lattice structure that mimics an eggshell.

video:video.video:title: Janet Jackson makes rare appearance at Grammy Hall of Fame Gala
video:video.video:description: Janet Jackson made a rare appearance at the 2026 Grammy Hall of Fame Gala, where her album "Rhythm Nation 1814" was inducted.

video:video.video:title: Saudi Arabia’s Interior Ministry opens its annual Hajj security parade ahead of pilgrimage season
video:video.video:description: Saudi Arabia’s Interior Ministry opens its annual Hajj security parade ahead of pilgrimage season

video:video.video:title: Halsey is turning her attention to film
video:video.video:description: Grammy-nominated artist Halsey explains why she's at the Cannes Film Festival, including to network for her freshly announced horror, "Replacer." (May 18)

video:video.video:title: Planned Parenthood: Goal of mail-order abortion pill ban is akin to a 'national abortion ban'
video:video.video:description: A maker of the widely used abortion pill mifepristone asked the Supreme Court to block an appellate court ruling that cut off mail-order access to the drug in what was the biggest jolt to abortion policy since the overturning of Roe v.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Health official in Ushuaia doubts Tierra del Fuego is source of suspected hantavirus outbreak
video:video.video:description: A health official in Ushuaia expressed doubts that Tierra del Fuego was the source of a suspected hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship with nearly 150 people on aboard, after three passengers died and at least three other people were left seriously ill.

video:video.video:title: San Diego police say they are responding to an active shooter at a local Islamic Center
video:video.video:description: San Diego police are responding to an active shooter at the Islamic Center of San Diego. Officer Anthony Carrasco says they believe people have been shot.

video:video.video:title: Federal court rejects Elon Musk's claims against OpenAI, saying he filed his lawsuit too late
video:video.video:description: A federal court on Monday dismissed claims filed against OpenAI and its top executives by Elon Musk, who accused them of betraying a shared vision for it to remain a nonprofit dedicated to guiding artificial intelligence’s development for the good of humanity.

video:video.video:title: Judge dismisses criminal charges against Kilmar Abrego Garcia, who was mistakenly deported
video:video.video:description: A federal judge has dismissed a human smuggling case against Kilmar Abrego Garcia, finding that the Justice Department’s pursuit of criminal charges was designed to punish him for challenging his mistaken deportation to El Salvador last year.

video:video.video:title: Mendes intimidated by scale of 'Masters of the Universe'
video:video.video:description: What surprised Camila Mendes the most when she arrived to film live action movie, "Masters of the Universe"?

video:video.video:title: Historic Maryland racetrack will host its final races, including Preakness, before shutting down
video:video.video:description: Once home to packed grandstands and stars like Secretariat, Laurel now draws only a few hundred regulars on many race days.

video:video.video:title: A political dynasty heiress and a former trade minister advance to Peru’s presidential runoff, final vote tally shows
video:video.video:description: Conservative Keiko Fujimori and progressive Roberto Sánchez will compete for Peru’s presidency in a June 7 runoff election, according to the final vote count from an election held more than a month ago.

video:video.video:title: Owl found in concrete mixer is on the mend and maybe a bit wiser from ordeal
video:video.video:description: An adolescent owl that was found stuck in a concrete mixer in southwestern Utah is finally on the mend, flying free and maybe a bit wiser from the ordeal.

video:video.video:title: French hantavirus patient has most severe cardiopulmonary form, doctor says
video:video.video:description: A French woman infected with hantavirus after being repatriated from the MV Hondius cruise ship has the "most severe form of cardiopulmonary presentation," a doctor at the Paris hospital treating her said Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Children in Gaza find relief from war trauma through breakdancing
video:video.video:description: Breakdance is helping Palestinian children in Gaza cope with the trauma of war. In the Nuseirat refugee camp, boys and girls from different ages gather every week to dance among the rubble, finding moments of relief and support by dancing.

video:video.video:title: Denver families dust off winter gear for spring snowstorm
video:video.video:description: A late spring snowstorm eased across parts of Colorado after closing schools, delaying flights and turning roads slushy on Wednesday. (AP Video by Thomas Peipert)

video:video.video:title: Trump says Marty Makary is out as Food and Drug Administration head
video:video.video:description: The head of the Food and Drug Administration, Dr. Marty Makary, is resigning after a rocky tenure that drew months of complaints from health industry executives, anti-abortion activists, vaping lobbyists and other allies of President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he doesn’t 'know' if he’s losing control of Senate Republicans
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump acknowledged he doesn’t "know” if he’s losing control of Senate Republicans, as GOP lawmakers appeared to abandon $1 billion in security funding for the White House ballroom complex and questions mounted over his administration’s $1.8 billion settlement fund.

video:video.video:title: Commuters welcome return of Long Island train service as deal reached to end strike
video:video.video:description: Trains are rolling again on the Long Island Rail Road after a deal ended a strike that shut down the nation’s busiest commuter rail system.

video:video.video:title: Guatemala's president denies report of US deal on anti-drug trafficking strikes
video:video.video:description: Guatemalan President Bernardo Arévalo on Thursday denied the existence of an agreement with the United States to conduct anti-drug trafficking operations on Guatemalan soil.

video:video.video:title: US-China summit ends with Xi and Trump claiming progress but differences remain
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump wrapped up his visit to Beijing Friday, flying back to Washington after a a private meeting at Chinese leader Xi Jinping's official residence.

video:video.video:title: Gunshots heard in the Philippine Senate as authorities tried to arrest a senator
video:video.video:description: A burst of gunfire rang out Wednesday night in the Philippine Senate, where authorities have tried to arrest a senator, who is wanted by the International Criminal Court for a charge of crime against humanity, an Associated Press journalist and other witnesses said.

video:video.video:title: Princess Catherine visits Italy on first solo trip abroad after cancer goes into remission
video:video.video:description: Britain’s Princess Catherine made her first overseas trip since announcing her cancer was in remission, traveling to Italy on Wednesday for a two-day tour focused on an early childhood educational approach that was developed here and exported globally.

video:video.video:title: After years of war, Sudan’s capital is left with thousands of unmarked graves
video:video.video:description: Three years of war have devastated much of Sudan, as The Associated Press' Fay Abuelgasim reports from the city of Omdurman, which sits on the western bank of the River Nile, opposite to the capital Khartoum.

video:video.video:title: Rio favela brings back street art tradition for World Cup
video:video.video:description: World Cup celebrations are already underway in Rocinha, Rio de Janeiro’s largest favela, on Tuesday, as local artists and residents painted an important commercial street.

video:video.video:title: New US postage stamps capture essence of historic Route 66
video:video.video:description: The U.S. Postal Service is releasing a new series of stamps to honor Route 66’s centennial. The offering includes eight stamps — one each for the states traversed by the Mother Road.

video:video.video:title: Timelapse of Muslim pilgrims circling the Kaaba in Mecca as Hajj begins
video:video.video:description: Around 1.6 million Muslims have gathered in Mecca ahead of the start of Hajj, one of the world’s largest annual religious gatherings, as pilgrims prepared for the beginning of the multi-day pilgrimage amid soaring temperatures and regional tensions.

video:video.video:title: Putin to visit China days after Trump met with President Xi
video:video.video:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin is traveling to China to meet with Chinese leader Xi Jinping less than a week after U.S.

video:video.video:title: Banksy confirms central London statue of man blinded by flag is his
video:video.video:description: A new sculpture in central London of a man striding off a plinth with his face blinded by a billowing flag was claimed Thursday by the elusive street artist Banksy, more than a day after it mysteriously appeared.

video:video.video:title: Adam Driver responds to Lena Dunham
video:video.video:description: Actor Adam Driver kept tight lipped when asked if he had a response for Lena Dunham after comments made about him by her in her book. (May 18)

video:video.video:title: Professional mourners mix tribal tradition with Kenya’s widespread Christianity
video:video.video:description: Loud and expressive mourners are common at funerals around the world—but in parts of Kenya, some of them may be paid workers.

video:video.video:title: Long Island Rail Road shuts down as workers strike
video:video.video:description: A marathon day of negotiations that finished early Monday failed to end a strike that shut down the Long Island Rail Road, North America’s largest commuter rail system, setting the stage for a rough start to the work week for about 250,000 commuters.

video:video.video:title: Naomi Osaka leaves hotel for Met Gala
video:video.video:description: Naomi Osaka stunned as she left The Mark Hotel for the Met Gala in a dramatic Robert Wun white sculptural fitted dress with exaggerated shoulders and adorned with red feathers and a matching headpiece.

video:video.video:title: A mosque and two vehicles are vandalized in the occupied West Bank as settler's violence intensifies
video:video.video:description: A mosque and two vehicles were vandalized in the occupied West Bank village of Jibiya early Friday, according to residents and the Palestinian Ministry of Religious Affairs, as settler's violence intensifies in the Palestinian territory.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 14
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, May 14th: Trump and Xi Jinping meet in Beijing for talks; Jerusalem braces for march with ultranationalists; Yemen and Houthi rebels agree to detainee swap; Newly restored tombs in Luxor are inaugurated.

video:video.video:title: Nearby resident recounts San Diego mosque shooting
video:video.video:description: Two teenage gunmen opened fire at an Islamic Center in San Diego on Monday and killed three men before killing themselves a few blocks away, police said.

video:video.video:title: A citizen campaign returns iconic kiwi birds to New Zealand's capital after century-long absence
video:video.video:description: The kiwi, New Zealand’s sacred national bird, vanished from the hills around Wellington more than a century ago, but now the capital's residents are waging an improbable citizen campaign to return the endangered flightless birds to the city.

video:video.video:title: Zoo in New Orleans welcomes rare Hartmann’s mountain zebras for the first time
video:video.video:description: Hartmann’s mountain zebra are listed as a species vulnerable to extinction due to habitat loss from livestock farming, climate-induced drought, and competition for water.

video:video.video:title: NYC residents could score $50 World Cup tickets through a new lottery
video:video.video:description: New York City residents will soon get a chance to buy $50 World Cup tickets through a city-run lottery, as officials try to make the tournament more accessible amid soaring ticket prices.

video:video.video:title: Taiwanese wary ahead of Trump-Xi summit amid concerns over island’s future
video:video.video:description: As U.S. President Donald Trump prepares for a high-stakes summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping this week, many in Taiwan are watching nervously, uncertain whether the island’s future could become part of negotiations between Washington and Beijing.

video:video.video:title: King Charles visits Jewish area of London hit by antisemitic attacks
video:video.video:description: Britain's King Charles III visited Golders Green on Thursday, an area of London which has seen a series of attacks over the past few months that have sparked fear and anger in Britain's Jewish community.

video:video.video:title: Mick Jagger breaks down the meaning of 'Rough and Twisted'
video:video.video:description: As The Rolling Stones announce the July 10 release of new album “Foreign Tongues,” Mick Jagger explains "Rough and Twisted," first released under an alias in April.

video:video.video:title: Funeral procession in southern Lebanon for Lebanese soldier killed in Israeli airstrike
video:video.video:description: Alaa Medlej was killed in an airstrike on a road near the village of Deir al-Zahrani, the army said.

video:video.video:title: New deal is fair for employees and does not raise prices for commuters, LIRR president says
video:video.video:description: New deal is fair for employees and does not raise prices for commuters, LIRR president says

video:video.video:title: Investigators searching home connected to killer of California college student Kristin Smart
video:video.video:description: Authorities entered the second day of their search Thursday at a home connected to the man convicted of killing 19-year-old college student Kristin Smart in 1996, according to law enforcement.

video:video.video:title: Timelapse video shows French aircraft carrier strike group transiting the Suez Canal
video:video.video:description: The nuclear-powered Charles de Gaulle is moving towards the Red Sea in preparation for a potential French-British mission in the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Buddhist hall housing 'eternal flame' burns down on Japan's Miyajima Island
video:video.video:description: A Buddhist hall in Western Japan known for housing an "eternal flame" that has been burning for over 1,000 years caught fire and burned to the ground on Miyajima Island.

video:video.video:title: Western states are increasingly using AI to spot wildfires
video:video.video:description: With a severe wildfire season forecast for the Western U.S., some communities are trying to get ahead of it using AI-monitored cameras.

video:video.video:title: Residents near Tenerife port feel divided ahead of arrival of hantavirus-hit cruise ship
video:video.video:description: Residents in Tenerife’s southern town of San Isidro voiced a mix of fear and sympathy Saturday as a cruise ship linked to a hantavirus outbreak prepared to arrive in the Canary Islands with more than 140 passengers and crew on board.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 9
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Saturday, May 9th: Frontier Airlines plane hits and kills pedestrian at Denver airport; Reports of fatalities in Lebanon after Israeli strikes; Hungary’s Péter Magyar sworn in as prime minister; 3,400-year-old ballgame survives in Mexico.

video:video.video:title: Man charged in White House correspondents' dinner attack pleads not guilty
video:video.video:description: A California man accused of storming the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner while armed with guns and knives has pleaded not guilty to charges he attempted to kill President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: US military strike on alleged drug boat in the eastern Pacific kills 1, leaves 2 survivors
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military launched another strike Tuesday on a vessel suspected of transporting drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing one man and leaving two survivors.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 23
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Saturday, May 23rd: Southern California chemical tank is at risk of failing; Family says Kyle Busch died after severe pneumonia progressed into sepsis; Protestor holds rally in Serbia's Belgrade; Flotilla activists describe beatings by Israeli forces.

video:video.video:title: Medical plane crashes and sparks fire in New Mexico mountain range
video:video.video:description: A small medical plane crashed in a mountain range outside Ruidoso, New Mexico, before dawn Thursday, killing all four people aboard and sparking a wildfire in the surrounding forest, officials said.

video:video.video:title: Boxing becomes an outlet for girls in Gaza
video:video.video:description: Under the blazing sun in Gaza’s Muwasi area of Khan Younis, girls of different ages line up between rows of tents to practice boxing, throwing punches at worn punching bags and makeshift training pads fashioned from plastic poles and padded tarps.

video:video.video:title: Spain's Sanchez accuses Israel and Netanyahu of 'illegal abduction' of Gaza flotilla activists
video:video.video:description: Israeli authorities said they were taking two high-profile activists who led an aid flotilla bound for Gaza, and who were captured by Israel in international waters of the Mediterranean Sea, to Israel for questioning.

video:video.video:title: Iran war, Taiwan and unresolved trade tensions all likely to feature on agenda for Trump-Xi meeting
video:video.video:description: Few expect major breakthroughs to the long-running frictions between China and the U.S., which range from competition in technology to the thorny question of Taiwan, whose main ally is the U.S.

video:video.video:title: 'Show me my daughter' cries a parent in anguish following deadly school fire
video:video.video:description: 'Show me my daughter!' a parent cried in anguish on Thursday following a deadly school fire in central Kenya, which killed at least 16 students, according to government officials.

video:video.video:title: Georgia marks Independence Day with military ceremony amid deep political polarization
video:video.video:description: The country became independent again in 1991 following the collapse of the Soviet Union.

video:video.video:title: Widow of FSU shooting victim is suing OpenAI, attorney calls ChatGPT a 'co-conspirator'
video:video.video:description: The lawsuit claims that Phoenix Ikner, the alleged gunman who was a 20-year-old FSU student at the time, used ChatGPT for "input and assistance" to carry-out the shooting that killed two people and wounded six others.

video:video.video:title: Spirit Airlines goes out of business after 34 years, ending operations immediately
video:video.video:description: Spirit Airlines, an impish upstart that shook the industry with its irreverent ads and deep discount fares, announced Saturday that it has gone out of business after 34 years.

video:video.video:title: The Hoover Dam gets new look to mark America 250
video:video.video:description: The Hoover Dam is marking the Semiquincentennial for the United States with a 300 foot tall flag and lighting to match.

video:video.video:title: Assault trial of former Patriots receiver Stefon Diggs begins with contrasting accounts
video:video.video:description: Former New England Patriots receiver Stefon Diggs’ former private chef testifies in his assault trial.

video:video.video:title: Kylie likes to turn everything off
video:video.video:description: Pop icon Kylie Minogue explains why you won't see much self-shot phone footage in new Netflix documentary "KYLIE."

video:video.video:title: Judge unseals purported Jeffrey Epstein suicide note
video:video.video:description: A note that Jeffrey Epstein 's former cellmate claims to have found after the financier's first suspected jail suicide attempt in 2019 has been made public.

video:video.video:title: Leaders gather in Yerevan for European Political Community meeting as Ukraine and Iran wars loom
video:video.video:description: The summit will be co-chaired by European Council President António Costa and Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan.

video:video.video:title: Events mark Missing and Murdered Indigenous Peoples awareness day
video:video.video:description: Across the country, Indigenous people are gathering this week to honor loved ones who are missing or have been killed and to call for better data collection, law enforcement response and reforms to make their communities safer.

video:video.video:title: Trump: 'I don't think about Americans' financial' woes during Iran talks
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he does not consider Americans’ financial strain “even a little bit” as he works to end the Iran conflict, comments that came as new data showed another sharp rise in consumer prices, driven in part by higher gas costs tied to the war.

video:video.video:title: Are fish and chips under threat in the UK?
video:video.video:description: For years, Brockley’s Rock in southeast London has been serving customers from its Lewisham shopfront.

video:video.video:title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang eats noodles on the streets of Beijing
video:video.video:description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang was spotted trying some of Beijing’s delicacies. He was seen at No. 69 Fangzhuanchang Noodles, tucking into a bowl of “zhajiangmian” — a Beijing specialty that features noodles covered in a thick soybean-paste sauce mixed with vegetables and meat.

video:video.video:title: Protest in Albania against work on luxury development linked to Ivanka Trump and Jared Kushner
video:video.video:description: Protesters in Albania clashed with private security guards at a site where a luxury development project linked to Ivanka Trump and Jared Kushner - the daughter and son-in-law of US President Donald Trump - is planned.

video:video.video:title: Israeli minister orders West Bank hamlet evicted after hearing he may face arrest warrant overseas
video:video.video:description: Israel's finance minister ordered the eviction of a Palestinian village in the Israeli-occupied West Bank that has long been a target of Israeli authorities on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Conan O’Brien speaks at Harvard commencement as Trump tightens pressure on the school
video:video.video:description: Harvard graduates heard from television host Conan O’Brien at their commencement. The appearance of O'Brien, a Harvard alum, comes as the Ivy League school faces mounting pressure from President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Hantavirus outbreak: 3 patients evacuated from cruise ship, new case confirmed in Switzerland
video:video.video:description: South African health authorities have identified the Andes strain of hantavirus in two passengers who were on a cruise ship at the center of an outbreak of the rare infection.

video:video.video:title: Police: Two suspects kill 3 people at a San Diego mosque before killing themselves
video:video.video:description: Authorities say two teenage suspects opened fire at an Islamic center in San Diego, California, killing three men before taking their own lives.

video:video.video:title: OC Fire official says chemical tank may have cracked potentially lowering explosion risk
video:video.video:description: A damaged chemical tank in Southern California may have cracked — potentially lowering the risk of a cataclysmic explosion — though an evacuation order remains in effect for residents with no timeline on when they can return.

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court restores access to abortion pill mifepristone through telehealth, opponents disagree
video:video.video:description: A supporter of the ban points to FDA "safeguards" in place when the drug was approved. (AP video: Joseph B. Frederick)

video:video.video:title: Rubio expects Trump to make further announcement after 'progress' on Iran deal
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Sunday that “significant progress, although not final progress, has been made” in negotiations over Iran as he met Indian External Affairs Minister Subrahmanyam Jaishankar during a four-day visit to India.

video:video.video:title: North Korea's Naegohyang Women’s FC and South Korea's Suwon FC train for AFC Champions League match
video:video.video:description: North Korea's Naegohyang Women's FC and its South Korean opponent Suwon FC Women spoke ahead of the women's football club competition in Asia.

video:video.video:title: Cannes and the Hôtel Martinez await a 'White Lotus' close-up
video:video.video:description: A look at The Hôtel Martinez, one of the iconic luxury hotels that line the beach in Cannes, which will be a central location for Season 4 of hit TV series, "The White Lotus."

video:video.video:title: Fashion expert weighs in on 2026 Met Gala looks and theme
video:video.video:description: Fashion writer Leigh Nordstrom of Women's Wear Daily highlights the most memorable looks and trends at the 2026 Met Gala including Beyoncé, Kim Kardashian and Eileen Gu.

video:video.video:title: Green card hopefuls could be forced to apply from abroad under Trump policy
video:video.video:description: Foreigners living legally in the U.S. who want a green card may soon have to leave the country and apply from abroad under a new Trump administration policy that has sparked confusion and concern among immigration lawyers and advocacy groups.

video:video.video:title: Village festival combines catching fish with clearing weeds and silt
video:video.video:description: Villagers wade through a spring covered with algae, clearing the weeds. It's a century old tradition in the Himalayan region of Indian-controlled Kashmir that combines environmental conservation with a community celebration.

video:video.video:title: Xi welcomes Putin for talks on economic cooperation and key international and regional issues
video:video.video:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin was welcomed by Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing Wednesday. Putin received a welcome by the honor guard, as well as young children waving Chinese and Russian flags.

video:video.video:title: Shakira opens up about her brightest and darkest moments
video:video.video:description: Shakira reflects on the brightest and darkest moments of her life. She'll perform alongside BTS and Madonna at the World Cup final halftime show in July.

video:video.video:title: Thailand's controversial former leader Thaksin Shinawatra released after eight months in prison
video:video.video:description: Thailand’s former Prime Minister Thaksin Shinawatra, whose 21st-century political odyssey sharply divided Thai society for decades, was released from a Bangkok prison on Monday after serving eight months of a one-year sentence for a corruption-related charge.

video:video.video:title: Waco police capture kangaroo on the loose in Texas
video:video.video:description: The Waco Police Department said officers captured a kangaroo that was loose Monday in Waco, Texas. After a short pursuit, the kangaroo, named Bingus, was safely captured and returned to a local wildlife rescue.

video:video.video:title: Hazen Fire burns more than 900 acres in rural Arizona
video:video.video:description: Firefighters battle the Hazen Fire burning Near Buckeye, Arizona; the fire sparked on Saturday afternoon is estimated at 980 acres

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court weakens the Voting Rights Act, hollowing out law that protects minority voting rights
video:video.video:description: Six decades ago, the Voting Rights Act became one of the most consequential laws in the nation’s history. On Wednesday, the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Mexican designer turns folk art into World Cup-inspired fashion
video:video.video:description: The project, created by Mexican design collective Mexclart, features T-shirts inspired by "papel picado," the delicate cut-paper decorations commonly seen during Mexican celebrations and holidays.

video:video.video:title: James Talarico kicks off general election by targeting Ken Paxton's scandals
video:video.video:description: Texas Democrat James Talarico launched his U.S. Senate campaign Wednesday by framing Republican opponent and Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton as part of the corrupt elite more than promoting his own policy agenda.

video:video.video:title: Israeli soldiers share rare accounts from Gaza, describing ongoing killings despite ceasefire
video:video.video:description: The Israeli combat soldier saw his teammates yelling in celebration, congratulating one another. They had just struck a vehicle of Palestinians driving near the Israeli-controlled part of the Gaza Strip, killing everyone inside.

video:video.video:title: Snow cover on Greek mountains halved in four decades according to new report
video:video.video:description: Snowfall is declining in Greece's mountains, leaving the communities that depend on it facing an uncertain future.

video:video.video:title: Federal charges allege $90 million stolen from Minnesota social service programs
video:video.video:description: The new charges against fifteen people were announced as the former leader of a Minnesota nonprofit was sentenced to nearly 42 years in prison for her role in a $250 million fraud case.

video:video.video:title: Ebola emerges as latest threat to displaced families in camps in eastern DRCongo
video:video.video:description: A conflict that has been raging for three decades has left millions of people displaced, some fleeing from one camp to another since they were born.

video:video.video:title: Rescue divers in Laos safely evacuate four villagers trapped in cave by floodwaters
video:video.video:description: Rescue workers in Laos said Saturday they have safely evacuated four villagers trapped in a flooded cave for 10 days, the day after another one was successfully extracted.

video:video.video:title: Live music stirring back to life in Tehran as ceasefire offers fragile respite
video:video.video:description: Bands and orchestras have begun returning to the stage in Tehran, where musicians have struggled to find work since war with the U.S. and Israel broke out in late February.

video:video.video:title: Oakland high school that produced black MLB greats invests in new players
video:video.video:description: The Oakland high school that produced Black MLB greats Frank Robinson, Curt Flood and Vada Pinson is determined to keep baseball alive — and its new head coach Jamey Smart is ready to help.

video:video.video:title: Rubio says Cuba has ‘always’ been a national security threat to the US
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio says Cuba has “always” been a national security threat to the United States.

video:video.video:title: Demi Moore: AI can never replace true art
video:video.video:description: Cannes Film Festival jury member Demi Moore gives her thoughts on the rise of AI in films.

video:video.video:title: Gunfire breaks out in Philippine Senate where authorities tried to arrest a senator
video:video.video:description: A burst of gunfire rang out Wednesday night in the Philippine Senate, where authorities have tried to arrest a senator who is wanted by the International Criminal Court for a charge of crime against humanity, an Associated Press journalist and other witnesses said.

video:video.video:title: Federal judge orders Louisiana to allow New Orleans exoneree to assume elected clerk office
video:video.video:description: A Louisiana exoneree who won election to a New Orleans clerk seat began work Monday after a federal judge temporarily blocked the state from eliminating the position.

video:video.video:title: May Day protesters block roadway at SFO's international terminal
video:video.video:description: Protesters blocked a road outside the international terminal at San Francisco’s airport on Friday, leading to the street's closure for about two hours.

video:video.video:title: Doctor identified among Pompeii victims thanks to analysis of the case he was carrying
video:video.video:description: One of the victims of the eruption that buried Pompeii in 79 AD. has been identified as a doctor thanks to analysis carried out on a small case that had remained hidden on them.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 13
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, May 13th: Trump in China to meet with Xi in Beijing; Prosecutors to retry Murdaugh after overturned murder convictions; Gunfire breaks out in the Philippine Senate; Tech improving the quality of life for dementia patients.

video:video.video:title: Ferrari unveils its first fully electric car
video:video.video:description: It's the luxury auto-manufacturer's first fully electric vehicle. It has four electric motors - one for each wheel, and a maximum estimated range of 530 kilometers.

video:video.video:title: Aircraft carrier USS Gerald R. Ford returns home after 11-month deployment
video:video.video:description: The USS Gerald R. Ford returned home to Virginia on Saturday after an 11-month deployment, the longest since the Vietnam War.

video:video.video:title: Trump plans dramatic makeover for Eisenhower office building
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump's proposal to put a coat of white paint on the exterior of a 19th-century historic landmark building next to the White House is slated for a hearing Thursday by a key federal agency he expects to approve what would be a dramatic makeover.

video:video.video:title: Nicolas Cage channels pop art, classic Hollywood in ‘Spider-Noir’
video:video.video:description: Nicolas Cage said he was inspired by pop artist Roy Lichtenstein and classic Hollywood actors as he developed “Spider-Noir.” (May 13)

video:video.video:title: Trump arrives in Beijing to meet Xi as war and inflation weigh on his presidency
video:video.video:description: US President Donald Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday for his highly anticipated summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping at a restless moment for a world worried about war, trade and artificial intelligence.

video:video.video:title: Lizzo promises 'good, clean fun' on new album
video:video.video:description: Lizzo says her new album is "good, clean fun" as she walks the carpet at the starry amfAR gala outside the Cannes Film Festival. (May 21)

video:video.video:title: Rep. Steve Cohen ending campaign in Tennessee after redraw of his Memphis district
video:video.video:description: Democratic Rep. Steven Cohen of Tennessee ends his bid for reelection, his career upended by the redistricting battles that are sweeping the country after last month’s Supreme Court decision.

video:video.video:title: Mayor Mamdani commits to safety and protecting right to protest at NYC Israel Day parade
video:video.video:description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani confirmed he will not attend this year's Israel Day parade but said he is committed to ensuring the event is safe and secure.

video:video.video:title: More than a dozen students killed in an overnight fire at a girls’ school in Kenya
video:video.video:description: At least 16 students died in an overnight fire that started in the dormitories of a girls’ boarding school in Kenya, a government official said, in the latest such incident to rock the East African nation.

video:video.video:title: Trump hosts UFC fighters ahead of White House event in June
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump summoned reporters to the Oval Office on Wednesday touting the upcoming Ultimate Fighting Championship match that will take place at the White House in June.

video:video.video:title: Kendrick Lamar, Dr. Dre and will.i.am attend Centennial High School groundbreaking ceremony
video:video.video:description: Kendrick Lamar returned to his alma mater Centennial High School in Compton, Calif., to attend a groundbreaking ceremony alongside Dr. Dre and will.i.am. (May 7)

video:video.video:title: Israel allows rare access to Yellow Line between Gaza area it controls and rest of territory
video:video.video:description: At an outpost in the central Gaza Strip, Israeli soldiers are enforcing what is known as the "yellow line”, the boundary between Israeli and Hamas-controlled areas of Gaza since the sides agreed to a ceasefire last October.

video:video.video:title: Two newly-restored tombs inaugurated in the West Bank of Luxor
video:video.video:description: Ancient wall paintings and inscriptions inside the Luxor tombs have been restored after years of conservation work.

video:video.video:title: Reaction in Taiwan to Trump's remarks on arms sales as bargaining chip for negotiations with China
video:video.video:description: There has been a mixed reaction in Taiwan to U.S. President Donald Trump's remarks on arms sales to the self-ruled island after hearing concerns about it from China's leader Xi Jinping.

video:video.video:title: Eurovision 2026 winner Dara describes what her song 'Bangaranga' means
video:video.video:description: Bulgarian singer Dara won the 70th Eurovision Song Contest on Saturday with her infectious party anthem “Bangaranga,” giving the southeast European country its first-ever Eurovision victory.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he'll 'be making decisions' on Taiwan arms package after Xi summit
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he has not yet decided whether a major sale of US arms to Taiwan can move forward following his three-day visit to China.

video:video.video:title: FBI Director Kash Patel denies drinking allegations in heated Senate exchange
video:video.video:description: FBI Director Kash Patel angrily lashed out at a Democratic lawmaker during a budget hearing Tuesday, calling allegations that he drinks excessively on the job and has been unreachable at times to his staff “unequivocally, categorically false.”

video:video.video:title: Learn the Eurovision dance moves
video:video.video:description: Eurovision 2026 contestants contestants teach the dance routines for their songs.

video:video.video:title: USPS considers allowing people to ship handguns through the mail
video:video.video:description: Handguns could soon be mailed through the United States Postal Service for the first time in nearly 100 years if a proposed rule under the Trump administration takes effect.

video:video.video:title: Gaza displaced mark Eid al-Adha amid deprivation in war-wrecked territory
video:video.video:description: Eid al-Adha is normally one of the most important Islamic holidays, marked by family gatherings, large meals and the ritual slaughter of livestock, with meat shared among relatives and those in need.

video:video.video:title: Red carpet rolls out for 79th Cannes Film Festival
video:video.video:description: The red carpet is out, ready to welcome stars to the 79th Cannes Film Festival which kicks off today.

video:video.video:title: Europe faces stray Ukrainian drones as Kyiv targets Russian oil exports
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian drones recently caused concern by entering Baltic airspace, leading to tensions with NATO countries.

video:video.video:title: Cristian Mungiu says 'Fjord' aims to entertain while encouraging tolerance
video:video.video:description: Cristian Mungiu says his Palme d’Or-winning film "Fjord" aims to entertain audiences while encouraging tolerance.

video:video.video:title: Rocket belonging to Jeff Bezos’ Blue Origin explodes during test at launch pad
video:video.video:description: Rocket belonging to Jeff Bezos’ Blue Origin explodes during test at launch pad

video:video.video:title: Virologist explains what to know about hantavirus
video:video.video:description: Giulia Gallo, Postdoctoral Scientist in the Viral Glycoproteins Group at The Pirbright Institute told The Associated Press that "the most important thing is not to create panic" as the transmission rate for the virus is very low.

video:video.video:title: Kevin Hart stays fearless in the face of his own roast
video:video.video:description: Comedy superstar Kevin Hart tells his roasters - including Deon Cole, Mo Amer, Tom Segura - to "have at it," as they arrive for the live taping of Netlfix's "The Roast of Kevin Hart."

video:video.video:title: Beef consumption in Argentina falls to lowest level in 20 years
video:video.video:description: Red meat consumption in Argentina — historically one of the world’s biggest consumers of beef — has fallen to its lowest level in two decades. (AP/ Cristian Kovadloff)

video:video.video:title: Amputee footballers in Rwanda find healing and a sense of community
video:video.video:description: Amputee football offers a sense of community and healing in Rwanda, a country that went through its darkest time in 1994 during its genocide.

video:video.video:title: Body camera video shows moment house explodes as NYPD responds to call
video:video.video:description: A massive, fiery explosion at a house in Queens sent several New York City police officers flying through the air early Thursday morning as authorities were responding to a call of a man inside armed with a knife and the smell of gas.

video:video.video:title: Wounded veterans snorkel with a whale shark at the Georgia Aquarium
video:video.video:description: The Georgia Aquarium invited injured veterans from the Wounded Warrior Project to snorkel with a whale shark and manta rays as part of their Military Salute Program.

video:video.video:title: May Day rally in New York targets Amazon
video:video.video:description: May Day demonstrators in New York speak out against Amazon, which they say has been pushing small business out of communities.

video:video.video:title: American passengers from virus-stricken cruise ship arrive in Nebraska and Georgia
video:video.video:description: American passengers from virus-stricken cruise ship MV Hondius arrived in Nebraska and Georgia, where they will receive care and monitoring at medical facilities.

video:video.video:description: Taiwan president Lai Ching-te is welcomed by Eswatini King Mswati III with a military ceremony at Lozitha Palace where they take part in a signing ceremony, bolstering ties with one of Taiwan's few remaining diplomatic allies despite growing international pressure from China.

video:video.video:title: Rubio says US President Trump is "very disappointed at NATO right now”
video:video.video:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is on his latest mission to assuage nervous U.S. allies in Europe about the Trump administration’s intentions with NATO or at least put a friendlier face on whipsawing changes and uncertainty about American troop reductions.

video:video.video:description: Three passengers have died and at least four people have been sick on board the MV Hondius, a Dutch cruise ship, in what health officials say is an outbreak of hantavirus, which usually spreads by inhaling contaminated rodent droppings.

video:video.video:description: Fire officials say two people were found dead in a plane after it crashed into a house in Ohio and exploded into flames.

video:video.video:title: $1 million bond set for livestreamer charged for allegedly shooting a man at Tennessee courthouse
video:video.video:description: A Tennessee judge has set a $1 million bond for a white livestreamer charged with attempted murder for allegedly shooting and wounding a Black man.

video:video.video:description: Finnish duo of singer Pete Parkkonen and violinist Linda Lampenius bring the heat to Eurovision in an act that involves jets of flame, a valuable 18th-century violin and a team of “ninjas” working to avert disaster.

video:video.video:description: The International Criminal Court has unsealed an arrest warrant for a prominent Philippine senator linked to the deadly “war on drugs” overseen by ex-president Rodrigo Duterte, which allegedly involved the extrajudicial killings of suspects.

video:video.video:description: Massie has been a thorn in the president’s side for pushing for the release of the Jeffrey Epstein files, opposing the war with Iran and voting against Trump’s signature tax legislation last year.

video:video.video:description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi and South Korean President Lee Jae Myung have emphasized the need for cooperation amid global challenges, including the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Views from inside the cruise ship after suspected hantavirus outbreak
video:video.video:description: A cruise ship with nearly 150 people aboard was waiting for help off the coast of Cape Verde in the Atlantic Ocean on Monday after three passengers died and at least three other people were left seriously ill in a suspected outbreak of the rare hantavirus, according to the World Health Organization

video:video.video:description: Indigenous leaders say U.S. government contractors in the rush to expand the wall are desecrating Native American sacred places and cultural sites at an unprecedented pace.

video:video.video:description: Jerusalem braced for tens of thousands of ultranationalist Jews to parade through Palestinian areas of the Old City on Thursday during an annual march that is often characterized by racist chants and violence.

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said on Saturday that he was reviewing a new Iranian proposal to end the war.

video:video.video:description: U.S. Senators are debating whether people who carried out violence during the Jan. 6, 2021 riot at the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Is the UK's traditional fish and chip supper under threat?
video:video.video:description: Britain’s traditional fish and chip supper is under pressure, from rising fish prices and tighter catch limits to the knock on effects of global conflict and higher energy costs.

video:video.video:title: Atlantic hurricane season forecast to be milder than normal thanks to El Nino
video:video.video:description: Meteorologists predict a developing El Nino could dampen the upcoming Atlantic hurricane season, but it won't eliminate storms.

video:video.video:description: Russia's President Vladimir Putin held a meeting with China's leader Xi Jinping in Beijing on Wednesday meant to reaffirm ties.

video:video.video:description: The new outbreak of a rare Ebola type in Congo and Uganda poses low global risk, the World Health Organization said Wednesday, but responders on the ground described being underprotected and undertrained for a rapidly spreading virus with no approved medicine or vaccine.

video:video.video:description: Two people were killed and at least 10 injured when a strike hit an area at Gaza City port Sunday, according to Shifa hospital that received casualties.

video:video.video:description: A woman who worked as a live-in chef for Stefon Diggs and is accusing the former New England Patriots wide receiver of assault is expected to continue testimony at his trial, which is marked by sharply conflicting accounts of what happened inside his home.

video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy published a video on Thursday that appeared to show him looking out of his plane window at the fighter jets his country hopes to receive.

video:video.video:title: Paris swelters in record May temperatures as France hit by heat wave
video:video.video:description: Paris seared under record May temperatures as an unseasonably strong heat wave continued in the French capital.

video:video.video:description: Florida Power & Light Company's mock hurricane drill simulates a response to a Category 2 hurricane named Aiden impacting the Vero Beach, Florida area.

video:video.video:description: A witness whose apartment overlooks the scene initially thought he was hearing construction equipment when the banging noises started.

video:video.video:title: Residents of Bulgaria capital are pleased following Dara's Eurovision victory and grand welcome home
video:video.video:description: Residents of Sofia reacted with excitement after Bulgarian singer, Dara, won the 70th Eurovision Song Contest on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 12
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, May 12th: Trump rejects Iran’s latest proposal to end war; Trump proposes suspending federal gas tax; Alabama could eliminate largely Black Congressional district; White House Correspondents Dinner suspect pleads not guilty.

video:video.video:title: Rubio meets Pope Leo on mission to ease tensions after Trump's criticism over Iran
video:video.video:description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio met Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican on Thursday. His visit was expected to be a fence-mending mission after weeks of tensions over the war in Iran in which US President Donald Trump has repeatedly bashed Pope Leo over his promotion of peace in the region.

video:video.video:title: Fans welcome Argentina team to Kansas City with cheers and songs ahead of World Cup matches
video:video.video:description: Cheering fans greeted reigning FIFA World Cup champions Argentina as the team arrived in Kansas City to begin preparing for its title defense.

video:video.video:description: Charli xcx poses on the 2026 Met Gala carpet

video:video.video:title: Iran war adds tension to Trump-Xi summit in China
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump is heading back to China, but this time, the Iran war raises the stakes for both sides. China is the bigger purchaser of Iranian oil.

video:video.video:title: Bulgaria welcomes home Eurovision winner Dara after historic victory
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of fans waving Bulgarian flags gathered at Sofia Airport as the singer arrived carrying the Eurovision trophy after winning with her song “Bangaranga.”

video:video.video:title: Trump orders banks to take a closer look at clients' citizenship in new immigration enforcement move
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order that requires banks to take a closer look at the citizenship of their customers, a new measure in his administration’s push to clamp down on people living in the country illegally.

video:video.video:title: SpaceX Starship rocket ignites into flames after landing in the Indian Ocean
video:video.video:description: SpaceX's Starship reached it's final destination — the Indian Ocean — despite some engine trouble, before erupting in flames upon impact.

video:video.video:title: Taylor Russell says playing an alien is a gift
video:video.video:description: Taylor Russell explains why she jumped at the opportunity to play an alien in Korean movie "Hope" by Na Hong-jin.

video:video.video:title: Jim Henson's puppet creature shop now open for tours
video:video.video:description: A puppet-making workshop founded by the legendary creator of the Muppets is drawing back its curtain. Jim Henson’s Creature Shop has opened its doors to public tours.

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court restores telehealth access to abortion pill mifepristone
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court on Monday restored broad access to the abortion pill mifepristone, blocking a ruling that had threatened to upend one of the main ways abortions are provided across the nation.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: SpaceX reveals plans for what could be the biggest-ever initial public offering
video:video.video:description: Elon Musk announced plans Wednesday for one of the biggest sales of stock to the public ever for his space company that is currently losing billions of dollars a year.

video:video.video:title: CNN founder Ted Turner, a pioneer of cable TV news, dies at 87
video:video.video:description: Ted Turner, a brash television pioneer who raced yachts, owned huge chunks of the American West and transformed the news business by launching CNN and introducing the 24-hour cable news cycle, died Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo calls for robust regulation of AI in manifesto which ponders the future of humanity
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV called Monday for robust regulation of artificial intelligence and for its developers to work for the common good rather than profit, issuing a sweeping manifesto on safeguarding humankind as the technology impacts everything from work to war.

video:video.video:title: Street artist JR begins inflating immersive cave on Pont Neuf bridge in Paris
video:video.video:description: Street artist JR and his team began inflating an immersive cave on the Pont Neuf bridge in Paris in the early hours of Thursday morning, in an exceptionally massive art installation in the ancient heart of the French capital.

video:video.video:title: Deon Cole reflects on Altadena benefit show
video:video.video:description: Deon Cole reflects on hosting a series of Netflix-sponsored stand-up comedy shows in Altadena as the community rebuilds after last year's deadly Eaton Fire.

video:video.video:title: Man accused of attacking OpenAI CEO's home pleads not guilty to attempted murder
video:video.video:description: The man accused of throwing a Molotov cocktail at the San Francisco home of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman pleaded not guilty to charges of attempted murder and attempted arson.

video:video.video:title: Riders put hobby horses through their paces at Germany's second championship
video:video.video:description: It was giddy up and go in Frankfurt on Saturday as the country's hobby horsers took part in their second ever championships.

video:video.video:title: Russia-China ties at 'unprecedented level,' Putin says ahead of China visit
video:video.video:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Russia-China ties have reached a “truly unprecedented level” in a video address released by the Kremlin on Tuesday, ahead of his visit to China for talks with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

video:video.video:title: What’s in the Pentagon’s newly released UFO files?
video:video.video:description: The Pentagon has begun releasing new UFO files under President Donald Trump’s push for “maximum transparency,” including videos, photos and reports of unexplained sightings from around the world.

video:video.video:description: A man accused of firing at least 70 rounds from a rifle on a busy street and wounding two drivers near Boston has pleaded not guilty to assault and other charges when he was arraigned from his hospital bed.

video:video.video:description: Workers on New York’s Long Island Rail Road are on strike, paralyzing the busiest commuter rail system in North America.

video:video.video:title: Israel and Lebanon agree to 45-day extension of ceasefire, US says
video:video.video:description: US officials says Israel and Lebanon have agreed to 45-day extension of ceasefire. It comes after two “productive” days of talks in Washington and will be followed by more negotiations June 2-3, said State Department spokesman.

video:video.video:description: Star Wars' Grogu is ready to take over the big screen in "The Mandalorian and Grogu."

video:video.video:title: Rubio on Iran: 'either going to be a good deal or there isn't going to be one'
video:video.video:description: Rubio’s visit to India marks an effort by Washington and New Delhi to steady strained relations as the two countries seek to reinforce strategic and economic ties despite recent trade tensions.

video:video.video:title: Maine lumber mill explosion injures at least 11 as firefighters respond to roaring blaze
video:video.video:description: A large fire and explosion at a lumber mill in Maine’s midcoast region has injured at least 11 people.

video:video.video:title: John Travolta dons beret in Cannes
video:video.video:description: In Cannes, John Travolta walks the carpet with the cast of his directorial debut, "Propeller One-way Night Coach," which is screening in the festival's Cannes Premiere section.

video:video.video:title: NY governor begs unions to resume talks as North America’s largest commuter rail system shut down
video:video.video:description: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul begged unions representing workers for the Long Island Rail Road, North America’s largest commuter rail system, to resume bargaining Sunday, saying the workers and hundreds of thousands of commuters stand to lose if a strike drags on.

video:video.video:title: What a hoot: Come meet the rescue owls in a Spanish sanctuary
video:video.video:description: A sanctuary in southern Spain is giving injured and rescued owls a second chance at life. Visitors can get unusually close to some of Europe's most striking birds of prey.

video:video.video:title: Oil tanker arrives in South Korea after passing through the Strait of Hormuz in mid-April
video:video.video:description: A South Korean refinery says a tanker carrying 1 million barrels of crude oil arrived off South Korea’s west coast after passing through the Strait of Hormuz in mid-April.

video:video.video:title: Molten lava spews into the sky as Hawaii's Kilauea Volcano experiences another eruption
video:video.video:description: Hawaii's Kilauea volcano shoots lava into the sky in the latest display of a powerful eruption, shooting molten rock into the surrounding area.

video:video.video:title: ‘KENREX’ turns a true crime into a one-man theatrical thriller
video:video.video:description: In search of the right true crime story, actor-writer Jack Holden says the 1981 Missouri killing of Ken Rex McElroy leant itself to a stage performance.

video:video.video:title: Advocates celebrate 230th anniversary of Ona Judge's escape from enslavement by George Washington
video:video.video:description: Ona Judge was 22 when she escaped from the president's Philadelphia residence on May 21, 1796. An exhibition about her and other enslaved people at Independence Mall, ordered removed by the Trump administration, has not been fully restored.

video:video.video:title: Cantona on soccer and the World Cup
video:video.video:description: As he promotes a documentary about his life, former soccer star Eric Cantona gives his opinion on VAR and explains why he won't be watching the upcoming FIFA World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Pen pal programs and letter writing are back in fashion among young people
video:video.video:description: Letter writing, like vinyls and analogue photography, is making a come back. In 2021, the U.S. Postal Service sent cards and envelopes to 25,000 elementary school classrooms for a pen pal project, but older students also are putting pen to paper

video:video.video:title: Lox Pratt says it's good to be bad
video:video.video:description: At age 14, actor Lox Pratt has already taken on two major bad guy roles in a row - as Jack in Netflix mini-series "Lord of the Files" and Draco Malfoy in the upcoming "Harry Potter" TV show.

video:video.video:title: 4 crew members eject safely after two Navy jets collide during air show in Idaho
video:video.video:description: All four crew members ejected safely after two Navy jets collided Sunday during an air show at the Mountain Home Air Force Base in western Idaho, officials said.

video:video.video:description: A North Korean women’s soccer team arrived in South Korea on Sunday for the semifinals of the AFC Women’s Champions League.

video:video.video:description: A train traveling at high speed hit a minibus crossing a railway in Belgium on Tuesday, killing four people, including two children, and badly injuring five other children in what officials called one of the worst rail accidents in the country's history.

video:video.video:description: At least five people were killed and 12 others injured after a Russian airstrike on Kramatorsk on Tuesday, local authorities reported.

video:video.video:title: US raises pressure on Cuba with indictment of former leader
video:video.video:description: Prosecutors announced criminal charges against former Cuban President Raúl Castro in the 1996 downing of civilian planes flown by exiles as the Trump administration escalated pressure on the island’s socialist government.

video:video.video:title: Miley Cyrus receives star on Hollywood Walk of Fame
video:video.video:description: Miley Cyrus says her Hollywood Walk of Fame star represents "an accumulation of devotion" and hopes her art "awakens something raw and imperfect and sexy and glamorous and joyful in times that need it."

video:video.video:title: Sinner: I 'hit the wall' in shocking French Open exit
video:video.video:description: No. 1 Jannik Sinner said he “hit the wall” after a shocking second-round exit at the French Open, despite entering the tournament as the overwhelming favorite to win the clay-court Grand Slam.

video:video.video:title: Why is everything so expensive? Business basics: Inflation
video:video.video:description: When the cost of nearly everything is rising, economists call it inflation. Even if paychecks also rise, inflation typically leads people feel like it's harder to make ends meet, and it often becomes a major political issue

video:video.video:title: France’s parliament votes to repeal slavery-era Black Code
video:video.video:description: The National Assembly voted 254-0 — a rare show of unanimity — to adopt a bill repealing the Code Noir, or Black Code, the 1685 decree King Louis XIV signed to govern slaves across France’s colonies.

video:video.video:description: Live calligraphy from Asia, modern Brit art and ships inside glass whales have all been curated for Hong Kong's Affordable Art Fair.

video:video.video:title: Iran wins friendly match against Gambia 3-1 in Turkey days before traveling to World Cup
video:video.video:description: Iran won a friendly match against Gambia 3-1 in Turkey on Friday just days before traveling to participate in the World Cup in the U.S., Mexico and Canada.

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrike targets Hamas leader in Gaza
video:video.video:description: An Israeli airstrike in Gaza on Friday targeted the leader of Hamas’ military wing, Israeli officials say, but it wasn't immediately clear if Izz al-Din al-Haddad was killed or injured.

video:video.video:title: What to know about the Ebola outbreak in Congo as WHO raises concerns
video:video.video:description: Health officials are raising alarm over a rare Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo that has caused more than 500 suspected cases and at least 134 suspected deaths, prompting the World Health Organization to declare a public health emergency.

video:video.video:title: Memphians head to state capitol to protest redistricting efforts
video:video.video:description: Galvanized by redistricting efforts that threaten to eliminate Tennessee's only majority-Black Democratic congressional district, Memphians waited in cold, rainy darkness Wednesday morning to board a bus that would take them to protest at the state capitol.

video:video.video:description: In Khartoum state, hastily buried bodies litter the city - buried in homes, in sports fields or by the road. Dr.

video:video.video:description: The Trump administration said from the start of the war with Iran that they undertook it to ensure that the country could never have a nuclear weapon.

video:video.video:description: Firefighters worked to contain a massive house fire in Norwood, New Jersey, on Tuesday afternoon. Aerial footage from WABC-TV shows the fire crew dousing the flames with water as large white and black plumes of smoke rise into the sky

video:video.video:description: Shrey Parikh has won the Scripps National Spelling Bee, beating Ishaan Gupta in a lightning-round tiebreaker. Shrey is a 14-year-old from Rancho Cucamonga, California.

video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, May 22nd: U.S. National Intelligence Director Tulsi Gabbard resigns; NATO allies confused by Trump ordering troops to Poland; 1.6 million Muslim pilgrims arrive in Saudi Arabia; and Cubans rally for former President Raúl Castro

video:video.video:description: A damaged chemical storage tank that authorities fear is at risk of exploding or rupturing in Southern California is gradually getting warmer despite efforts to reduce its internal temperature, a fire chief said Saturday.

video:video.video:description: “The Migrant Child” by street artist Banksy was unveiled Thursday after restoration work and is now touring Venice’s canals for public display.

video:video.video:description: Russia on Thursday unleashed a third straight day of massive drone and missile attacks on Ukraine, demolishing an apartment building in Kyiv where nine people were killed and dozens injured, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: WHO chief gives update on Ebola epidemic in Congo and Uganda
video:video.video:description: The head of the World Health Organization said Friday that the Ebola outbreak in Congo is rapidly spreading and now poses a “very high” risk at the national level.

video:video.video:description: Three people were injured in a Russian strike on the south eastern Ukraine city of Zaporizhzhia on Friday, the State Emergency Service said.

video:video.video:title: Expert: Federal gas tax brings benefits; worries suspension could become permanent
video:video.video:description: President Trump proposed suspending the federal gas tax that funds highway infrastructure to ease spiking prices at the pump.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 3

video:video.video:title: Alex Murdaugh's double-murder convictions are overturned
video:video.video:description: The South Carolina Supreme Court on Wednesday overturned the murder convictions and life sentence of disgraced lawyer Alex Murdaugh in the shooting deaths of his wife and younger son.

video:video.video:title: Biotech entrepreneur Vivek Ramaswamy wins Ohio’s GOP nomination for governor
video:video.video:description: Billionaire biotech entrepreneur Vivek Ramaswamy has clinched the Republican nomination for Ohio governor and will face off this fall against the state’s COVID-era health director, Democrat Amy Acton.

video:video.video:title: Bolivia’s capital under siege as protests and blockades deepen crisis for President Paz
video:video.video:description: La Paz has been cut off after nearly three weeks of blockades by protestors demanding the resignation of President Rodrigo Paz in the face of the economic crisis gripping the Andean nation.

video:video.video:title: IDL brings global crews together in high-stakes battle
video:video.video:description: Connor Lim discusses his International Dance League, which brings together global dance crews in high-stakes battles. (May 12)

video:video.video:title: Experts to study sites visited by hantavirus-infected couple in South America's Tierra del Fuego
video:video.video:description: Argentina’s Health Ministry says that a team of scientific experts investigating the origin of the hantavirus outbreak in Argentina will leave for the country’s southernmost city of Ushuaia at the end of this week or beginning of next.

video:video.video:title: Students killed in an overnight fire at a girls' school
video:video.video:description: At least 16 students died and dozens were injured in an overnight fire that started in the dormitories of a girls’ boarding school in Kenya, a government official said.

video:video.video:title: Amtrak may ease gun restrictions on trains despite security concerns
video:video.video:description: Amtrak is considering allowing passengers to store guns in lockboxes on most trains, raising security concerns after an alleged attempt on President Donald Trump’s life involving a rail traveler.

video:video.video:description: Hamas confirmed in a statement on Wednesday the killing of Odeh, a senior commander, who they said was killed on Tuesday along with his wife and two of his children in an Israeli airstrike that targeted a residential building in Gaza City.

video:video.video:title: Africa CDC chief says he's in 'panic mode' over Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda
video:video.video:description: The absence of approved therapeutics or vaccines for the Bundibugyo virus, a rare variant of the Ebola disease, has caused concerns among health authorities in Africa amid the active cases.

video:video.video:title: Giant drills set to bore new train tunnels to New York City
video:video.video:description: The giant drills that will bore tunnels under the Hudson River for trains traveling to NYC are being assembled across the river in New Jersey. (AP Video: Ted Shaffrey)

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 29

video:video.video:description: The girls were arrested for planning and carrying out a suspected arson attack at Utumishi Girls School in central Kenya, according to the Directorate of Criminal Investigations, or DCI, a department of the national police.

video:video.video:description: A man stabbed and wounded three people at the train station in the Swiss city of Winterthur on Thursday before being arrested, police said.

video:video.video:description: The captain of the hantavirus-stricken ship thanked his crew and passengers for their 'unity' and 'strength' in a video message released on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Scripps National Spelling Bee returns
video:video.video:description: The Scripps National Spelling Bee is back this week. The bee began in 1925, with this year marking its 98th edition.

video:video.video:title: North America’s largest commuter rail system shuts down as workers strike
video:video.video:description: The Long Island Rail Road, North America’s largest commuter rail system, was shut down Saturday after unionized workers went on strike for the first time in three decades.

video:video.video:description: Barney Frank, the longtime Democratic congressman and leading liberal who brought new visibility to gay rights and crafted the most significant reforms to the financial system in a generation, has died.

video:video.video:description: Across makeshift camps, daily life is defined by scarcity, exposure and exhaustion. Tents are torn and unfit for any season, offering little protection from winter rain or the harsh summer heat.

video:video.video:description: Prosecutors have filed criminal charges against the operator of the ship that crashed into Baltimore’s Francis Scott Key Bridge leading to the deaths of six construction workers in March 2024.

video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met Tuesday with Bahrain’s King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa, proposing a drone defence partnership and discussing shared security threats including Iranian strikes and tensions around the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:description: Five villagers who became trapped in a flooded cave in central Laos over a week ago have been found alive by divers who discovered them sitting on a rock in the darkness, rescuers said Wednesday, but two others are still missing.

video:video.video:description: About 40,000 people were under evacuation orders and schools shut down Friday in Southern California after a storage tank continued to leak a hazardous chemical used to make plastic parts that officials said could rupture or explode.

video:video.video:description: Mick Jagger, Ronnie Wood and Keith Richards talk about upcoming Rolling Stones album, "Foreign Tongues," before a New York audience including Leonardo DiCaprio. (May 6)

video:video.video:description: Director Nicolas Winding Refn broke down and cried during a press conference in Cannes while talking about his near-death experience.

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump suggested the U.S. may not move forward with an Iran deal unless more Gulf nations join the Abraham Accords, saying some countries “owe that to us" and should normalize ties with Israel as negotiations with Tehran continue.

video:video.video:description: Award-winning actor Sir Derek Jacobi is 87 years old, but he doesn't plan on retiring any time soon.

video:video.video:description: Small vessels were seen carrying people from the MV Hondius to the quayside at Granadilla port. Officials were waiting there to guide them to buses that would transfer them to a medical facility for quarantine.

video:video.video:description: Worshippers from around the world have gathered in Islam’s holiest city amid regional conflicts and tightened restrictions. (AP Video: Baraa Anwer and Altaf Qadri)

video:video.video:description: Residents in Beirut’s busy Hamra neighborhood said people rushed to follow the news and monitor for possible evacuation warnings after Netanyahu announced more intensive strikes targeting Hezbollah across Lebanon.

video:video.video:description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez said Wednesday that his party is cooperating with police, amid an ongoing investigation into possible financial wrongdoing.

video:video.video:description: The attack took place in Lakki Marwat, a district in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province, local police chief Azmat Ullah said.

video:video.video:description: Three Israeli drone strikes on vehicles just south of Beirut on Saturday killed four people, while a series of airstrikes on southern Lebanon killed at least 13, including a man and his 12-year-old daughter, state media and the Health Ministry said.

video:video.video:description: Elon Musk announced plans for one of the biggest stock sales ever by taking public a space company that is currently losing billions of dollars a year.

video:video.video:title: Dead humpback whale dragged to Danish shore
video:video.video:description: The carcass of a humpback whale whose life and death captivated Germans for months was dragged onto a Danish beach on Saturday after spending two weeks in shallow waters off the island of Anholt.

video:video.video:description: Behind closed doors, Xi told Trump during a meeting that, if Taiwan is handled well, U.S.-China relations “will enjoy overall stability".

video:video.video:description: Unconfirmed reports of local media said that a defence enterprise, local college, shopping centre and residential buildings were damaged.

video:video.video:title: Rami Malek on his musical abilities
video:video.video:description: Rami Malek did not consider himself a musical person — until he starred in "Bohemian Rhapsody." His new film is "The Man I Love."

video:video.video:title: Russian drone attack in Ukraine's Ternopil city wounds 12, local officials say
video:video.video:description: The Ukrainian city of Ternopil was targeted by at least 36 drones on Friday wounding at least 12, the regional military administration said in a statement.

video:video.video:title: Annual pilgrimage to the El Ghriba Synagogue on the island of Djerba
video:video.video:description: The annual Jewish pilgrimage to the El Ghriba Synagogue drew a modest but notable return of foreign visitors this year, as hundreds gathered under tight security nearly three years after a deadly attack disrupted the centuries-old tradition.

video:video.video:title: Rubio says Iran claims of not wanting a nuke are not backed up by actions
video:video.video:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Tuesday that Iran must make a choice between war and peace but that peace will require a convincing demonstration that the Iranian government won’t attempt to pursue atomic arms.

video:video.video:title: Experts warn of rising lead risks in Africa’s solar energy boom
video:video.video:description: Years after a toxic battery recycling plant shut down in Kenya’s coastal city of Mombasa, residents of Owino Uhuru village said they are still living with the scars of lead poisoning, a warning, experts say, of a growing public health risk as Africa’s clean energy boom drives a surge in battery use.

video:video.video:title: Man describes getting shot at Oklahoma party
video:video.video:description: Authorities in Oklahoma were looking for suspects Monday in a weekend shooting at an unsanctioned lakeside party packed with young adults that left at least 23 people injured, some critically, according to police and hospital officials.

video:video.video:title: Unable to perform, Cuban artist struggles to reinvent himself as crisis deepens

video:video.video:title: Delta Goodrem is more than ready for the Eurovision stage

video:video.video:title: 'This is not another COVID': WHO chief seeks to reassure after arrival in Tenerife
video:video.video:description: As a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship is set to arrive at Granadilla port in Tenerife, Spain on Sunday morning, the WHO, Spanish authorities and cruise company Oceanwide Expedition are coordinating the disembarkation of passengers and some crew on ground.

video:video.video:title: In Bunia’s Ebola fight, women are on the front lines often without protective gear
video:video.video:description: In Bunia, at the heart of the Ebola response, health facilities are reporting a severe shortage of protective and support equipment as they grapple with a surge in suspected cases, primarily among women.

He was seen at No. 69 Fangzhuanchang Noodles, tucking into a bowl of “zhajiangmian” — a Beijing specialty that features noodles covered in a thick soybean-paste sauce mixed with vegetables and meat.
