video:video.video:description: Helene Goessaert, a passenger of a cruise ship at the center of a rare-virus outbreak, says that the atmosphere on board the ship is 'relatively good'.

video:video.video:title: In Sudan’s war economy, gold keeps flowing as miners risk mercury and collapse
video:video.video:description: The men carried metal detectors as they scanned a mountainous area in northern Sudan in search of gold.

video:video.video:title: New wave of gang violence in Haiti displaces hundreds of people
video:video.video:description: A new wave of gang violence in Haiti’s capital forced hundreds to flee their homes over the weekend, leaving families scattered along the road to the country’s main airport on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Eight people injured in Russian attack on Sumy, Ukraine's emergency service says
video:video.video:description: Eight people were injured as a result of a Russian strike on Sumy, the state emergency service said.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo to spend first anniversary of his election as pope in Naples
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV is spending the first anniversary of his election as Pope in Naples, where he'll pray at a sanctuary devoted to the Madonna.

video:video.video:title: Think it's warm now? The next five years look even record-breaking hotter, UN weather agency warns
video:video.video:description: A new World Meteorological Organization report gives a three-out-of-four chance that the next five years will average more than 1.5 degrees Celsius above pre-industrial temperatures.

video:video.video:title: Families scour southern Mexico after 40 migrants vanished on a dangerous sea route
video:video.video:description: Relatives of dozens of missing migrants travelled to Mexico’s southern Pacific coast to search for clues about family members who disappeared while attempting a maritime journey toward the United States.

video:video.video:title: Isabel Allende gives 'The House of the Spirits' TV adaptation her stamp of approval
video:video.video:description: Best-selling author Isabel Allende has given Prime Video's "The House of the Spirits" her stamp of approval.

video:video.video:title: WHO expert says Hantavirus outbreak 'is not the next COVID'
video:video.video:description: A top epidemic expert at the U.N. health agency said Wednesday that the hantavirus outbreak is not the next COVID pandemic.

video:video.video:title: Dozens of artists bring new life to gigantic former ironworks on UNESCO's world heritage list
video:video.video:description: 50 urban artists from 17 countries converge on one of Europe's most important industrial landmarks between May and November for a show that takes advantage of the former ironworks' sprawling spaces and aura of abandonment.

video:video.video:title: Croak monsieur! Australian shopper finds live frog in grocery store salad
video:video.video:description: When Australian farmer Rhys Smoker announced he’d found a live frog in a bag of lettuce, his housemates didn’t believe him.

video:video.video:title: UK's largest chalk hill figure, the Cerne Abbas Giant, gets a makeover
video:video.video:description: At 180 feet high, the giant is etched on the hillside above Cerne Abbas, a village in Dorset, England.

video:video.video:title: Foxhounds kick off horse races at the Iroqouis Steeplechase with howling fun dog parade
video:video.video:description: At the Iroquois Steeplechase, the horses are competing for the top purses, but the fan favorite is the parade of the hounds.

video:video.video:title: Caregiving robot helps disabled American couple without home support
video:video.video:description: Caregiving robots are helping people living with disabilities with daily tasks from exercising to taking medication.

video:video.video:title: Access to abortion pills under fire
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court this week restored broad access to the abortion pill mifepristone, blocking a lower-court ruling that had threatened to upend one of the main ways abortions are provided across the nation.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Parents mourn their son as a town in Argentina faces its own hantavirus outbreak
video:video.video:description: Daisy Morinigo and David Delgado said they initially thought their 14-year-old son had the flu when he came down with a fever and body aches.

video:video.video:title: Details emerge of potential Iran deal which would reopen Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: The United States is close to reaching a deal with Iran that would end the war, reopen the Strait of Hormuz and see Iran give up its stockpile of highly enriched uranium, with the details and timelines to be worked out later, regional officials told The Associated Press on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: Thousands gather in London for rival protests as police separate opposing groups
video:video.video:description: Tens of thousands of protesters descend on London on Saturday for two major demonstrations.

video:video.video:title: Delta Goodrem is more than ready for the Eurovision stage
video:video.video:description: Australia is fielding "Born to Try" star Delta Goodrem as its entry for the 70th Eurovision Song Contest, where she will compete with "Eclipse." (May 11)

video:video.video:title: Suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship raises questions about source and spread
video:video.video:description: A rodent-borne illness is suspected of causing an outbreak aboard a cruise ship that has killed three people and sickened others.

video:video.video:title: Cubans struggle to survive on pocket-sized government ration book as products dwindle
video:video.video:description: Cuba's pocket-sized government ration book has been circulating for more than six decades. It used to offer a bounty of products, but that offer has dwindled in recent years as the island's economic crisis deepened.

video:video.video:title: Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship arrives at Tenerife in Spain's Canary Islands
video:video.video:description: The ship won't dock but will remain anchored, with people ferried off in small boats. Everyone disembarking will be checked for symptoms, and will only be taken off the ship once evacuation flights are ready to fly them to their destinations.

video:video.video:title: Connor Storrie arrives at Met Gala
video:video.video:description: Connor Storrie at the 2026 Met Gala.

video:video.video:title: Senior Iran official warns that war could expand and asks opponents to 'turn back from this path'
video:video.video:description: Gen. Mohsen Rezaei, a senior advisor to Iran's supreme leader, warned on Sunday that the Iran war could expand the include the areas of the Red Sea, Bab el-Mandeb, and the Indian Ocean.

video:video.video:title: Shakira's free concert on Copacabana beach draws 2 million fans
video:video.video:description: The performance followed similar shows by Madonna in 2024 and Lady Gaga last year — also attended by huge crowds that danced on the sprawling sands.

video:video.video:title: Deaths reported after tank implodes at Washington pulp and paper mill
video:video.video:description: Authorities say an undisclosed number of people have been killed and or remained missing after the implosion of a chemical tank at a pulp and paper mill in southwestern Washington state on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Sudan's war has left thousands missing. Many are buried in unmarked graves
video:video.video:description: It was a classic husband's phone call. He was done for the day and would stop at the market before coming home. But he was returning from war, not work.

video:video.video:title: Residents of Lithuania's capital told to shelter as drone alarm underlines NATO's eastern jitters
video:video.video:description: Residents of Vilnius were told to take shelter, and airspace over Vilnius Airport was closed for about an hour on Wednesday after a suspected drone was detected near the Belarus border.

video:video.video:title: WHO chief Tedros 'deeply concerned about the scale and speed' of Ebola outbreak
video:video.video:description: The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak a public health emergency of international concern on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: Invalides monument in Paris: 350 years of care for wounded soldiers and war victims
video:video.video:description: World-famous as the resting place of Napoleon, the gilded dome of the Invalides in Paris has drawn millions of visitors, but behind the landmark’s grand façade was a lesser-known mission: serving as a home and hospital for wounded soldiers and victims of war for more than 350 years.

video:video.video:title: Muslims celebrate Eid al-Adha across the world
video:video.video:description: A joyous occasion celebrated by Muslims around the world, Eid al-Adha marks Prophet Ibrahim’s test of faith and his willingness to sacrifice his son in submission to God.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds of flotilla activists land in Turkey, recount abuse and torture by Israeli authorities
video:video.video:description: Wearing grey sweatsuits and Arab keffiyehs, the activists descended the stairs to the runaway in Istanbul flashing two-fingered salutes and chanting “Free Palestine.”

video:video.video:title: Ahead of World Parrot Day, Koki the cockatoo celebrates his 70th birthday
video:video.video:description: It's not unusual for cockatoos in captivity to live to a grand old age, but Koki - Croatia's best-known parrot - has an unusual history.

video:video.video:title: South Korean Starbucks boss apologizes for ad campaign that evoked massacre
video:video.video:description: South Korean retail tycoon Chung Yong-jin on Tuesday issued his second apology in two weeks as Starbucks’ local operation faces a backlash over a recent marketing campaign that was widely perceived as mocking victims of a bloody military crackdown on pro-democracy protesters in 1980. ( AP video sho

video:video.video:title: Two killed in Israeli strike on civilian vehicle in Gaza, health officials say
video:video.video:description: Two people were killed and others wounded after an Israeli airstrike hit a vehicle in Gaza City on Saturday evening, according to local health officials.

video:video.video:title: Iran war energy shock raises Asia's interest in fuel blending
video:video.video:description: India is pushing for the use of more biofuels in transportation in order to slash dependence on imported fossil fuels, which have been disrupted by the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Most active volcano in the Philippines spews giant ash column sky high
video:video.video:description: The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology said the alert level remained at 3, based on its latest 24-hour monitoring.

video:video.video:title: Three hantavirus patients evacuated from cruise ship off Cape Verde
video:video.video:description: Three patients were evacuated from a cruise ship off Cape Verde on Wednesday amid a hantavirus outbreak, as a new case was confirmed in Switzerland.

video:video.video:title: Muslims in India and Bangladesh join Eid al-Adha prayers
video:video.video:description: In India people gathered in the Jama Masjid Mosque, while worshippers in Bangladesh gathered at the capital's National Eidgah.

video:video.video:title: South Carolina redistricting proposal moves to Senate after House approval
video:video.video:description: The South Carolina House on Wednesday approved a resolution giving lawmakers permission to return later, after their regular work ends, to redraw congressional districts that could eliminate the state’s only Democratic-held district.

video:video.video:title: Trump shows reporters ballroom construction site as lawmakers balk at $1B for security
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump is defending the White House ballroom he's building, showing off the construction site to a group of reporters on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: 'A moment of sacrifices': Italian farmers wrestle with economic fallout of Iran war
video:video.video:description: Since Israel and U.S. attacked Iran on Feb. 28, diesel for agricultural vehicles in Italy has risen from 0.85 euros ($1) per litre to nearly 1.50 ($1.76), while fertilizer ingredients have gone up by 35%.

video:video.video:title: Aftermath of Russian strikes on Kyiv with drones and ballistic missiles
video:video.video:description: Russia targeted Ukraine's capital with a mass drone and missile attack early Thursday morning that killed at least one and injured 33 people, local authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Inflatable art turns The Hague into a giant open-air museum
video:video.video:description: The city center of The Hague has been transformed into a vibrant open-air museum with the arrival of inflatable art.

video:video.video:title: Tehran residents welcome easing of internet shutdown imposed after start of war
video:video.video:description: Iranian authorities have eased a monthslong internet shutdown that they cast as a wartime necessity, but that has cost the country's economy an estimated $30-$40 million a day.

video:video.video:description: Six people were injured in a Russian strike on Ukraine's northeastern city of Sumy on Thursday, the State Emergency Service of Ukraine said.

video:video.video:title: Your climate impact doesn't end when you die. More people are considering 'greener' death options
video:video.video:description: As interest in alternative forms of burial has risen in recent years, some have turned to green, or conservation burials, to reduce the environmental impact of their passing, such as at the Prairie Creek Conservation Cemetery outside Gainesville, Florida.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian drone pilots turn military exercise in Sweden into critical warning for NATO
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian drone pilots turned a military exercise in Sweden into a critical warning for NATO. The pilots, playing the role of the enemy in the exercise, said they destroyed Swedish forces with drones — and that if the exercise had been a real life scenario, the Western forces would have been dead.

video:video.video:title: Walk in the shoes of a Met Gala attendee

video:video.video:title: How to decide when to get a mammogram

video:video.video:description: Parents’ groups in France on Tuesday called for more attention to long-ignored child abuse scandals as a rare public trial opened of a school assistant accused of sexually assaulting nine small children in Paris.

video:video.video:description: Farmers already stressed by the war at home between Sudan's military and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces are now watching costs rise due to war in the Middle East.

video:video.video:description: The villagers entered the cave in Xaisomboun province on May 19, but heavy rain triggered flash flooding that blocked the exit, according to Lao and Thai rescue teams involved in the operation.

video:video.video:description: Residents of Al-Mughayyir, a village in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, said settlers had set fires to their agricultural land the night before and fired flares along the western edge of the village.

video:video.video:description: Presidents Xi Jinping and Donald Trump met on Thursday in Beijing for a U.S.-China summit where few breakthroughs are expected on divisive issues ranging from the Iran war, trade, technology and Taiwan.

video:video.video:description: Xi Jinping welcomes Donald Trump at his official residence, Zhongnanhai, on Friday for the final talks of the summit.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 24
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Sunday, May 24th: U.S. is close to a deal with Iran to end the war; Pakistan train bombing kills at least two dozen people; China investigates deadly coal mine blast; Kenton Cool may climb Everest again after record 20th ascent.

video:video.video:description: Robert Downey Jr. and his producer wife Susan Downey aren't ready to confirm that a third "Sherlock Holmes" film is in the works, calling it "the greatest mystery."

video:video.video:description: Paying tribute to Zanardi, Italy's Premier Giorgia Meloni said on X that Italy had lost "a great champion and an extraordinary man".

video:video.video:description: Nicolas Cage reveals the inspirations behind new TV series “Spider-Noir."

video:video.video:description: Thousands of fans gathered Thursday evening in Mexico City's central square to watch a free live performance of the Chilean television show "31 Minutos" to mark Children’s Day.

video:video.video:description: For more than a century, only conservators and experts could catch a close-up glimpse of early works by Austrian artist Gustav Klimt in Vienna.

video:video.video:description: Richard Glossip was released from incarceration for the first time in nearly 30 years Thursday after posting bond while awaiting retrial for a 1997 killing that put him on the brink of execution three separate times.

video:video.video:description: Cannes Film Festival is underway, with 12 days of nonstop premieres and fabulous red carpet looks from stars including Demi Moore, Jane Fonda and Diane Kruger. (May 15)

video:video.video:description: An invasive aquatic plant has spread rapidly across Colombia’s Cienaga Grande de Santa Marta over the past year.

video:video.video:title: Experts urge ethical debate as cosmetic procedures become the norm
video:video.video:description: As cosmetic interventions become more pervasive, people are grappling with the philosophical and ethical implications of turning to them.

video:video.video:description: Soaring costs for fuel due to the Iran war are leading panicked consumers in hard-hit Asia toward rooftop solar power, a likely windfall for China as the world’s largest provider of solar technology.

video:video.video:description: A new exhibition at London’s Natural History Museum shines a light on Jurassic marine life and asks what their extinction could teach us about protecting the oceans now.

video:video.video:description: The indictment was related to Castro’s alleged role in the shootdown of two small planes operated by the exile group Brothers to the Rescue. (AP video by Cody Jackson)

video:video.video:title: Japanese woman who scaled the world's 14 top peaks wants to share joy
video:video.video:description: The first woman to have three times conquered the world's second-highest peak, K2, has also summited the world's 14 highest peaks.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo expects visit from Rubio as Trump continues to throw barbs at Pontiff
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio will meet with Pope Leo on Thursday. The visit was first seen as an attempt to improve relations with the Trump administration following a series of attacks by U.

video:video.video:title: Tornadoes cut across Mississippi as severe storms damage homes
video:video.video:description: Powerful storms that spawned at least three tornadoes tore through several Mississippi counties. Authorities say the storms damaged nearly 500 homes, knocked down trees and power lines, and injured at least 17 people.

video:video.video:title: Robert Downey Jr. says live theater is 'vital'
video:video.video:description: Robert Downey, Jr. says live theater is "vital" as he and his wife, producer Susan Downey, are honored at Lincoln Center Theater's annual gala. (May 18)

video:video.video:description: Pedro Pascal on collaborating with stunt performers and body doubles Lateef Crowder and Brendan Wayne to create Din Djarin, his Disney+ series character now on big screens in "The Mandalorian and Grogu."

video:video.video:title: Xi and Putin highlight their friendship and cooperation on energy and other issues in Beijing visit
video:video.video:description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin have hailed their strategic ties and energy trade during a meeting in Beijing.

video:video.video:title: Memphis communities split by redistricting
video:video.video:description: After Tennessee lawmakers voted to separate Memphis into three congressional districts, Memphians fear they'll no longer have a representatives who understand and care about their communities.

video:video.video:title: Revelers pack a Hong Kong island for a bun festival, a century-old tradition
video:video.video:description: At midnight in Hong Kong’s tiny outlying island of Cheung Chau, 12 contestants raced up a 14-metre bamboo tower covered with 9,000 plastic buns.

video:video.video:description: The World Health Organization warned Friday that the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo is spreading rapidly, as officials race to contain the virus amid insecurity and population displacement in the country’s east.

video:video.video:title: Baby pygmy hippo takes first outdoor bath at Berlin Zoo
video:video.video:description: A baby pygmy hippopotamus at Berlin Zoo took its first outdoor bath this week after being born on May 9.

video:video.video:title: Trump administration claims its war in Iran ended with April ceasefire, AP Explains
video:video.video:description: The Trump administration is arguing that the war in Iran has already ended because of the ceasefire that began in April, an interpretation that would allow the White House to avoid a federal law requirement for congressional approval of military action that extends beyond 60 days.

video:video.video:title: Driver crashes into pedestrians in northern Italy, injuring eight people

video:video.video:title: Inauguration ceremony for new Patriarch of Chaldean Catholic Church in Iraq
video:video.video:description: Patriarch Mar Polis III Nona - born Amel Shamoon Nona - previously served as the Archbishop of Mosul and the Archbishop of Sydney before becoming the patriarch for the Chaldean Catholic Church.

video:video.video:title: Footage captures attack on Nun near Jerusalem’s old city

video:video.video:title: UK's mystery chalk hill figure, the Cerne Abbas Giant, gets a makeover
video:video.video:description: The outline of the UK's largest chalk hill figure, the Cerne Abbas Giant, is getting a revamp with 17 tonnes of wet chalk.

video:video.video:title: Trump-Xi summit has high stakes for Taiwan
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump has demonstrated greater ambivalence toward Taiwan in his second term. That's raising questions about his commitment to maintaining American support for the self-ruled island ahead of next week's summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping.

video:video.video:title: Israeli fighter jets strike southern Lebanese village after warning its residents to leave
video:video.video:description: Israel's fighter jets struck a southern Lebanese village Friday shortly after warning its residents to leave the area.

video:video.video:title: Lawyers for the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk ask to seal evidence and parts of a key hearing
video:video.video:description: Lawyers for the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk make their case to bar reporters and the public from parts of a key upcoming hearing and seal some evidence after the judge rejected their request to ban news cameras from the courtroom.

video:video.video:title: NYC Mayor announces affordable World Cup tickets to be made available for some lucky residents

video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump wrapped up his visit to Beijing Friday after a private meeting at China's leader Xi Jinping's official residence and departed for Washington.

video:video.video:description: The strike shutting down North America’s largest commuter rail system will continue into a third day.

video:video.video:description: The lead Democrat on the House Judiciary Committee is calling the Trump administration's creation of a $1.7 billion fund to compensate allies of the Republican president who believe they were mistreated by the Biden-era Justice Department a "slush fund."

video:video.video:description: Tensions have been increasing in southern Lebanon, where Israeli troops in recent days have crossed the strategic Litani River, which the Israeli military has used as a de facto boundary.

video:video.video:title: M23 rebels in Goma play down Ebola fears as WHO delegation visits treatment center and lab
video:video.video:description: A M23 rebel group spokesperson downplayed concerns about the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo as a team from the World Health Organisation visited a treatment centre in Nyiragongo Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Pediatricians say schools need to make time for recess for kids of all ages
video:video.video:description: For the first time in more than a decade, a leading pediatricians group has put out new guidance about recess, saying it’s crucial for good health and good grades.

video:video.video:title: Scott Bessent says steps have been taken to put Trump on a $250 bill
video:video.video:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Thursday he doesn't think "there's anything untoward" about President Donald Trump's face appearing on the $250 bill in honor of the 250th anniversary of the United States, but he said the final decision is up to Congress.

video:video.video:title: Pop-up arcade game in DC mocks Trump administration's handling of Iran war
video:video.video:description: The mini arcade is the latest piece from Secret Handshake, a resistance art group that has installed several pieces of artwork on the Mall to protest the Trump administration, including a Golden toilet.

video:video.video:title: Health workers battle Ebola as normal life continues in Bunia, eastern DR Congo
video:video.video:description: People in the eastern Democratic Republic of Congo city of Bunia appear to be going about life as usual, even amid a rapidly spreading Ebola outbreak.

video:video.video:title: Trump invites Xi to the White House during state dinner in Beijing
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump extended a Sept. 24 invitation to Chinese President Xi Jinping and his wife, Madame Peng, during his reciprocal toast at a state dinner in the Great Hall of the People in Beijing.

video:video.video:title: Asylum seekers deported by US detained in Equatorial Guinea hotel
video:video.video:description: Under an opaque $7.5 million deal with the Trump administration, Equatorial Guinea’s all-powerful president has turned a hotel owned by his family into a prison for asylum seekers deported from the United States.

video:video.video:title: Australian Jews tell antisemitism inquiry of surge in hate before Bondi Hanukkah massacre
video:video.video:description: All witnesses called to give evidence on Monday were Jewish Australians who recounted their experiences of hatred, some speaking under pseudonyms out of fear for their safety.

video:video.video:title: Clashes erupt in Bolivia as protesters allied with ex-leader Morales march on capital
video:video.video:description: Followers of Bolivia’s influential ex-President Evo Morales on Monday joined a massive protest movement fueled by the nation’s worst economic crisis in a generation.

video:video.video:title: Amid confrontation with the US, an Iranian museum exhibits anti-war art by American artists
video:video.video:description: While Tehran's streets had lined with anti-American billboards and posters amid the tension in the Middle East, Iranians turned out to visit an anti-war exhibition at one of the top museums.

video:video.video:title: What to know about the New Jersey ICE facility protests and hunger strike
video:video.video:description: Protesters have clashed with armed federal immigration officers in front of a New Jersey detention center where advocates have asserted detainees are staging a hunger strike.

video:video.video:title: Kendrick Lamar, Dr. Dre and will.i.am attend school groundbreaking ceremony
video:video.video:description: Kendrick Lamar returned to his alma mater Centennial High School in Compton, Calif., to attend a groundbreaking ceremony alongside Dr. Dre and will.i.am.

video:video.video:title: Digital workers in Gaza try to earn badly needed incomes and find a form of escape from adversities
video:video.video:description: Digital freelancing became popular more than a decade ago in Gaza. But traditional sectors shrank after Hamas seized control of the strip in 2007, as Israel’s intensified blockade devastated agriculture, manufacturing and other industries.

video:video.video:title: French luxury brands celebrate 250 years of Franco-American relationship with new exhibit
video:video.video:description: A new exhibit titled “Hidden Treasures” shines a light on the 250 year cultural dialogue between France and the U.S. through the lens of French luxury goods.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 4
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, May 4th: U.N. mission on a task force on opening the Strait of Hormuz has been postponed; fire breaks out at Broadway theater; authorities seek suspects in lakeside shooting in Oklahoma; fish and chip prices on the rise in the UK

video:video.video:title: Alabama legislators and residents protest special session to redraw voting districts
video:video.video:description: U.S. Rep. Terri Sewell showed solidarity with local legislators at a rally outside Alabama's State House. (AP Video by Kim Chandler)

video:video.video:title: 'Fast & Furious' cast reunite at Cannes Film Festival
video:video.video:description: Vin Diesel, Meadow Walker and the 'Fast & Furious' stars celebrated the 25th anniversary of the franchise with a special midnight screening. (May 13)

video:video.video:title: Damson Idris walks the 2026 Met Gala carpet
video:video.video:description: Damson Idris wears leather on the 2026 Met Gala carpet.

video:video.video:title: Documents show Queen Elizabeth was eager for ex-Prince Andrew to become trade envoy
video:video.video:description: The late Queen Elizabeth II was “very keen” for former Prince Andrew to be named Britain’s trade envoy in 2001, according to documents released Thursday that showed his appointment received little scrutiny from government ministers.

video:video.video:title: Under President Milei's austerity, disabled Argentines risk losing essential services
video:video.video:description: In recent months, the Argentine government has frozen payments to organizations providing therapeutic and educational services to people with disabilities.

video:video.video:title: Biden sues Justice Department to stop release of interview audio tied to special counsel probe
video:video.video:description: Joe Biden sued the Justice Department to block the release of audio recordings and transcripts of the former president's interview with a ghostwriter that were obtained by the special counsel who investigated his handling of classified documents.

video:video.video:title: Muslims from around the world gather in Mecca for Hajj pilgrimage
video:video.video:description: Around 1.6 million Muslims have gathered in Mecca on Sunday ahead of the start of Hajj, one of the world’s largest annual religious gatherings.

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrikes hit southern Lebanon as troops capture Beaufort Castle near Nabatiyeh
video:video.video:description: Lebanon’s state-run National News Agency reported that Israeli warplanes carried out multiple airstrikes across villages in southern Lebanon on Sunday, causing casualties, though it did not provide a breakdown of the victims.

video:video.video:title: Iran soccer team departs for Turkey training camp ahead of World Cup
video:video.video:description: Iran's semi-official Tasnim news agency posted a video showing members of the country's soccer team signing Jerseys ahead of their trip.

video:video.video:title: Some Japanese snack packages are turning black-and-white as Iran war depletes ink supply
video:video.video:description: The packaging on some snacks in Japan is turning a somber black-and-white, as the war in Iran disrupts the supply of an ingredient used in colored ink.

video:video.video:title: Paxton dominates Cornyn in Texas US Senate runoff, the latest sign of Trump’s hold on GOP
video:video.video:description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton won the Republican nomination for U.S. Senate on Tuesday, easily defeating four-term Sen.

video:video.video:title: Could your next job interview be with an AI bot?
video:video.video:description: Imagine taking part in a job interview not conducted by a human, but an AI avatar... What might seem dystopian in concept is an increasing reality for some applicants.
